ItemFacetWriteRequest.description:
Write serializer for ItemFacet (POST create) — input validation only.

The viewset drives the crafting service directly; this serializer parses
and validates the ``item_instance`` and ``facet`` foreign keys.

ItemInstanceRead.description:
Read serializer for ItemInstance — used by the inventory listing.

Also backs ``VisibleItemDetailViewSet`` (looking at another character's
worn item) — the ``can_steal`` field is only meaningful there.

suggested_value.description: Appraised worth in coppers — a market pricing suggestion (#2243).

display_image_url.description: Return the cloudinary URL for the item's display image, if any.

is_usable.description:
True iff use_item would proceed: the template has an on-use pool.
Delegates to the canonical ``ItemTemplate.is_usable`` predicate.

is_open.description: Whether this item is currently open.

is_currency_instrument.description:
True iff ``obj`` is a minted coin (loose cache or grand coin, #1909).

Gates the Deposit affordance client-side. A boolean, not the nested
``CurrencyInstrumentDetails`` — the client only needs to know whether
to render the button, not the denomination/face-value breakdown.

can_steal.description:
True iff the viewer could steal ``obj`` right now (#1909).

Visibility = eligibility: delegates to the same ``steal_permitted``
predicate the ``steal`` service re-checks at execution time. No
viewer in context → False (IC reads scope to the active character) —
this is the case for the plain inventory list/retrieve endpoints,
where the viewer is always looking at their own items anyway.
``VisibleItemDetailViewSet`` passes the observer's ``CharacterSheet``
as ``viewer_sheet`` so looking at someone else's worn item can
surface the affordance.

ItemStyleWriteRequest.description:
Write serializer for ItemStyle (POST create) — input validation only.

The viewset drives the crafting service directly; this serializer parses
and validates the ``item_instance`` and ``style`` foreign keys.

ItemTemplateDetail.description: Detail serializer for ItemTemplate with slots and interactions.

description.description: Default full description when examined. Instances can override.

size.description: Size value for container nesting. Smaller items fit inside larger containers.

is_container.description: Whether this item can hold other items.

container_capacity.description: Maximum number of items this container can hold.

container_max_item_size.description: Maximum size of items that fit inside this container.

is_stackable.description: Whether items of this type can be stacked.

max_stack_size.description: Maximum quantity in a single stack.

is_consumable.description: Whether this item has limited uses/charges.

is_craftable.description: Whether this item can be crafted by players.

supports_open_close.description: Whether instances can be opened/closed (coats, bags, lockets).

size.description: Size value for container nesting. Smaller items fit inside larger containers.

is_container.description: Whether this item can hold other items.

is_stackable.description: Whether items of this type can be stacked.

is_consumable.description: Whether this item has limited uses/charges.

is_craftable.description: Whether this item can be crafted by players.

KnownSecret.description: One known secret, from the viewer's side, with locked layers shown as "Unknown".

LabStationDetails.description: Read-shape for LabStationDetails — status endpoint + write-endpoint bodies.

durability.description: Current wear-remaining before the station is broken.

max_durability.description: Durability ceiling for this station's current level.

LabStationRepairResult.description:
Response shape for ``repair``.

Only the two durability fields ``RepairLabStationAction.execute()``
actually returns (``actions/definitions/room_features.py``) —
``level``/``is_broken`` are NOT part of this response, unlike the
``GET`` status endpoint (``LabStationDetailsSerializer``).

LibraryEntry.description: Read-only serializer for library browse cards.

tier.description:
Severity tier 1 (cosmetic) through 5 (body partially remade).

* `1` - Cosmetic Touch
* `2` - Marked
* `3` - Touched
* `4` - Marked Profoundly
* `5` - Remade

social_reactivity_magnitude.description: Reaction strength from magic-phobic observers. Calibrated as situational world-friction, not character-concept blocker.

is_visible_at_rest.description: Shows through normal clothing? Required True at tier 4+.

LocationModeEnum.description:
* `anywhere` - Anywhere
* `anchor` - Anchor Room
* `rooms` - Authored Rooms
* `instance` - Spawned Instance
* `area` - Target Area

MachineMatchLifecycleStateEnum.description:
* `ALIVE` - Alive
* `CAPTURED` - Captured / Unknown
* `COMA` - Coma
* `RETIRED` - Retired
* `DEAD` - Dead

ManageTrainingAddRequest.description: Validate input for creating a training allocation.

ManagerBuilding.description: Building header: identity, style, and the space budget meter.

ManagerExit.description: One directed exit between two rooms of the building.

ManagerRoom.description: One room in the manager payload (id = the room's ObjectDB pk).

ManagerTenancy.description: An active tenancy row, as the building owner sees it.

MentorPersona.description: Minimal read-only representation of a mentor persona.

MissionAbandonResult.description: Result of the #1023 abandon endpoint — the run's id and new status.

MissionCategory.description:
List + detail serializer for MissionCategory browse.

Read-only resource exposed via MissionCategoryViewSet; categories
are seeded via fixture/admin, not authored through the API.

display_order.description: Browse ordering in the authoring tool (lower = earlier). No Meta.ordering on the model — callers order explicitly via ``order_by('display_order', 'name')``.

MissionGiver.description:
Staff CRUD for trigger-based MissionGiver rows (#729).

Covers the two surviving GiverKind variants (ROOM_TRIGGER,
ENVIRONMENTAL_DETAIL). ``templates`` is a flat M2M draw pool — each
template self-gates at draw time via its own ``availability_rule``
(Option A), so there are no per-attachment overrides here.

giver_kind.description:
How this giver reaches the player; selects the target's expected typeclass. ROOM_TRIGGER → Room; ENVIRONMENTAL_DETAIL → examinable item (auto-grants one); BOARD → examinable item that lists many postings (preview-then-take, #2044).

* `environmental_detail` - Environmental Detail
* `room_trigger` - Room Trigger
* `board` - Notice Board

target.description: The Evennia object this giver is bound to. Its typeclass must match giver_kind: NPC → Character-typeclass; ROOM_TRIGGER → Room-typeclass; ENVIRONMENTAL_DETAIL → any non-Character/Room/Exit Object (an examinable item or room detail). Null = draft (see is_publishable). All FK targets land in ObjectDB; the kind enum + clean() typeclass check enforce semantic shape without the wasted nullable columns of a discriminator.

org.description: Optional organization this giver fronts for (used by ORG arc-scope).

templates.description: The mission templates this trigger giver offers (#729). A flat draw pool — each template self-gates at draw time via its own availability_rule, so per-attachment overrides aren't needed (Option A).

MissionGiverRequest.description:
Staff CRUD for trigger-based MissionGiver rows (#729).

Covers the two surviving GiverKind variants (ROOM_TRIGGER,
ENVIRONMENTAL_DETAIL). ``templates`` is a flat M2M draw pool — each
template self-gates at draw time via its own ``availability_rule``
(Option A), so there are no per-attachment overrides here.

* `board` - Notice Board

MissionInstance.description:
Staff-side serializer for MissionInstance (assign + remove surfaces).

Read-only for the common fields; the staff-assign action wraps the
`staff_assign_mission` service to populate these (so authors don't
POST to this endpoint to create instances — assign is a deliberate
operator gesture).

current_node.description: Where the run currently sits; null = complete.

is_paused.description: Set when a participant disconnects (#1899) — see maybe_pause_mission_for_disconnect.

source_beat.description: Optional: the stories Beat this run resolves. When set, the stakes contract locks at acceptance (activate_stakes_for_instance) and the Beat completes at terminal (on_mission_complete_for_beat). SET_NULL on Beat delete. For offer-issued runs, set from MissionOfferDetails.source_beat at issue_mission (#1780).

target_project.description: Optional: the Project this run advances (#2045). Copied from MissionOfferDetails.target_project at issuance (mirrors source_beat). SET_NULL on Project delete — a cancelled project unbinds the run; report-time payout soft-skips with a notice.

MissionInstanceStatusEnum.description:
* `active` - Active
* `resolved` - Resolved (awaiting report)
* `complete` - Complete
* `abandoned` - Abandoned
* `expired` - Expired

MissionInviteRequestRequest.description:
POST body for the #887 invite endpoint.

``invitee_character_id`` is the ObjectDB pk of the character to invite
(web clients speak character ids). The view resolves it to that
character's primary ``Persona``.

MissionInviteRespondRequest.description:
POST body for the #887 respond endpoint.

``invite_id`` is the ``MissionInvite`` pk; ``response`` is the literal
``"accept"`` or ``"decline"``.

MissionNode.description:
Editor CRUD for MissionNode rows.

``allowed_riders`` exposes the consequence M2M as a list of PKs (the
authoring UI passes them through unchanged). Editor layout fields
(editor_x / editor_y) round-trip; flavor_text and its needs_rewrite
sibling are both editable. ``location_mode``/``locations``/``target_area``
round-trip the node's location gate (#885, #888).

key.description: Stable per-template node key (unique within the template).

conflict_mode.description:
How contested option choices resolve for multiple participants.

* `group_vote` - Group Vote
* `joint` - Joint

joint_combine.description:
JOINT mode: how participant results combine.

* `any` - Any
* `all` - All
* `count` - Count

joint_count.description: JOINT+COUNT mode: minimum number of successes required.

allowed_riders.description: Reusable consequence riders permitted at this node. NOT consumed by the engine in Phase A; reserved for future use.

deny_all_riders.description: When true, no consequence riders may attach at this node. NOT consumed by the engine in Phase A; reserved for future use.

editor_x.description: Mission Studio (Phase E) canvas X coordinate. Pure authoring metadata — no engine meaning. IntegerField (negatives allowed) so authors can pan to negative coords.

editor_y.description: Mission Studio (Phase E) canvas Y coordinate. Pure authoring metadata — no engine meaning. IntegerField (negatives allowed) so authors can pan to negative coords.

flavor_text.description: Thin abstract description of the moment shown to the player when they enter this node (design §8.2). Paragraph-style (TextField, unbounded) — the short per-option label is the option's authored_ic_framing (CharField/200). The rich narration is the player-authored Legend Entry; this is just the engine's framing line.

flavor_text_needs_rewrite.description: Phase-D copy service sets True (inherited copy reads as 'rewrite me'); the Phase-D edit service clears it on save. Surfaces in the Studio's 'N flavor fields are still flagged as un-rewritten copy' counter (design §10). NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service responsibility.

location_mode.description:
Default location gate for this node's options (#885): ANYWHERE = live wherever the character is; ANCHOR = live only in the instance's grant-time anchor room; ROOMS = live in this node's authored ``locations`` set. An option with its own ``locations`` rows overrides this default.

* `anywhere` - Anywhere
* `anchor` - Anchor Room
* `rooms` - Authored Rooms
* `instance` - Spawned Instance
* `area` - Target Area

locations.description: Authored rooms where this node's options are live (consulted only when ``location_mode=ROOMS``). Options may override per-option via ``MissionOption.locations``.

target_area.description: Target area for AREA location_mode. A room matches when its RoomProfile.area is this area or any descendant via AreaClosure.

MissionOfferDetails.description:
Staff CRUD for mission-kind offer details (#728).

``role`` is denormalized from ``offer.role`` by the model's ``save()``
override (per #686 Phase 6) so callers never set it directly — it's
read-only here, and the unique-constraint promise on
``(role, mission_template)`` is enforced at the DB level.

role.description: Denormalized from offer.role to enforce (role, mission_template) catalog uniqueness. Kept in sync via save().

source_beat.description: Optional: the staked Beat this offer resolves. When set, accepting the offer copies it onto MissionInstance.source_beat, arming the #1770-PR4 stakes gate + contract activation. Independent of Beat.required_mission (ADR-0010). SET_NULL on Beat delete.

target_project.description: Optional: the live Project this offer's missions advance (#2045). When set, accepting the offer copies it onto MissionInstance.target_project at issuance — exactly how source_beat works. SET_NULL on Project delete (a cancelled project unbinds the offer; issuing then refuses).

weight.description: Per-offer weight override for POOL draw. Null falls back to MissionTemplate.base_weight.

requirements_override.description: Predicate JSON AND-composed with MissionTemplate.availability_rule and NPCServiceOffer.eligibility_rule at evaluation time. Empty dict = no additional gate.

role_cooldown_duration.description: Duration written into NPCRoleCooldown on accept (blocks OTHER missions on the role for this persona). Null falls back to MissionTemplate.cooldown.

draw_priority.description: POOL-draw priority tier (#726). The highest tier present is drawn first with guaranteed inclusion (up to the pool count); lower tiers fill any remaining slots via the weighted draw. Give chain-unlock / high-stakes follow-up missions a positive value so they surface ahead of the general pool. 0 = general pool.

MissionOption.description:
Editor CRUD for MissionOption rows (authored or challenge-sourced).

Both source_kind values are editable; consumer code distinguishes via
the kind field (BRANCH vs CHECK). visibility_rule is a JSONField that
rides through; the predicate-tree builder API (D5) is what authors
actually use to write it.

order.description: Display/evaluation order within the node (no Meta.ordering — callers order explicitly).

visibility_rule.description: Phase 0 predicate tree gating this option's visibility.

authored_check_type.description: AUTHORED+CHECK: the check resolved by this option.

authored_ic_framing.description: Short option-row label shown in the choice list (CharField/200). Keep terse; the longer 'what happens at this node' narration belongs in MissionNode.flavor_text.

authored_ic_framing_needs_rewrite.description: Phase-D copy service sets True (inherited copy reads as 'rewrite me'); the Phase-D edit service clears it on save. Surfaces in the Studio's 'N flavor fields are still flagged as un-rewritten copy' counter (design §10). NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service responsibility.

branch_target.description: BRANCH/authored route: the node this option leads to.

challenge.description: CHALLENGE source: the challenge whose approaches fan out into this option's challenge-contributed options at runtime. on_delete=PROTECT — detach all referencing options before deleting the challenge.

MissionOptionRoute.description: Editor CRUD for MissionOptionRoute rows (one per outcome tier per option).

outcome_tier.description: Resolved outcome tier; null = the single BRANCH route.

is_random_set.description: When true, destination is drawn from weighted candidates.

consequence.description: Authored structured effect applied when this route's outcome tier is rolled; null = pure routing/no effect.

outcome_text.description: Player-facing outcome text shown when this route's tier is rolled (design §8.3). WIRED by #885 — the resolution engine surfaces it as the actor's STORY message (a random-set route prefers the chosen candidate's outcome_text, #941).

outcome_text_needs_rewrite.description: The copy service sets True on cloned text; the editor serializer clears it when the text is rewritten (#941). NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service/serializer responsibility.

consequence.description: Optional per-candidate consequence override; falls back to the parent route's consequence when null. STORED BUT UNCONSUMED in Phase B — Phase D wires per-candidate emission.

outcome_text.description: Optional per-candidate outcome text shown to the player. STORED BUT UNCONSUMED in Phase B — Phase D wires it.

outcome_text_needs_rewrite.description: Phase-D copy service sets True; the Phase-D edit service clears it on save. NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service responsibility.

MissionOptionRouteReward.description:
Editor CRUD for reward lines attached to a route OR a candidate (XOR).

Model-level CheckConstraint + clean() both enforce exactly-one-parent.
DRF's ModelSerializer skips clean() by default, so we mirror the XOR
check here as ``validate()`` to surface a clean 400 instead of letting
the DB constraint raise IntegrityError → 500.

route.description: Parent route (route-level reward). Exactly one of route / candidate must be set; enforced in clean().

candidate.description: Parent candidate (per-candidate reward bundle — design §8.3). STORED BUT UNCONSUMED in Phase B; Phase D wires emission when a random candidate fires. Exactly one of route / candidate must be set.

kind.description:
When the emitted line pays out (IMMEDIATE/POST_CRON/PROPAGATION).

* `immediate` - Immediate
* `post_cron` - Post Cron
* `propagation` - Propagation

sink.description:
Which ledger the emitted line pays into.

* `money` - Money
* `legend_points` - Legend Points
* `resonance` - Resonance
* `rumor` - Rumor
* `crime_watch` - Crime Watch
* `beat` - Beat
* `item` - Item
* `summons` - Follow-On Summons
* `project` - Project

amount.description: Numeric magnitude of the broadcast reward, when applicable.

followon_offer.description: Required when sink=FOLLOW_ON_SUMMONS: the MISSION-kind offer whose summons fires as the follow-on reward.

followon_message.description: IC text for the follow-on summons when sink=FOLLOW_ON_SUMMONS. Empty = the summons shows the offer's default text.

followon_expiry_hours.description: When sink=FOLLOW_ON_SUMMONS: the summons lapses after this many hours if unanswered (triggering the refusal/escalation path). Null = no expiry (the summons persists until accepted or declined).

contract_holder_only.description: True → emit exactly one line to the instance's contract holder, regardless of how many participants ran the mission. False → distribute per the template's reward_group_rule.

MissionTemplate.description:
List + detail serializer for MissionTemplate browse.

Read-only fields cover the authoring footprint: name, summary,
epilogue, level band, risk tier, weighting, era association, scope,
cooldown, reward-group rule, active flag, visibility, categories,
availability rule.

The visibility flip is a straight write (#870): there is no
"publish to nobody" failure mode to guard against — a RESTRICTED
template whose rule admits no PC simply IS staff-only, a valid
emergent state. ``availability_rule`` IS validated (well-formedness;
a malformed tree would crash every later availability check).

summary.description: Rich IC opening lore (mission bookend).

base_weight.description: Relative weight in the availability draw.

created_in_era.description: Arc association — the era this mission was authored for.

arc_scope.description:
Whether this is offered globally, per-org, or per-giver.

* `global` - Global
* `org` - Organization
* `giver` - Giver

percent_replace.description: Percent chance this template replaces an existing offer (0-100).

cooldown.description: Per-giver re-offer cooldown. Must be non-negative.

reward_group_rule.description:
Multi-participant payout split (authoring knob only; actual distribution-by-rule is Phase 5).

* `all_equal` - All Equal
* `by_role` - By Role
* `by_participation` - By Participation

visibility.description:
Audience gate (#870): OPEN surfaces the template to everyone (availability_rule is not consulted); RESTRICTED makes the availability_rule predicate the eligibility gate — an empty rule admits no PC (the emergent staff-only / in-testing state, and the production-safe default for new templates). Staff (is_staff_observer) always bypass.

* `open` - Open
* `restricted` - Restricted

categories.description: Content-type tags for this mission (multi-valued, e.g. assassination, courtly, heist). Drives browse/filter in the authoring tool.

availability_rule.description: Phase 0 predicate tree gating front-door availability for this template.

MissionTemplateDetail.description:
Detail response: list fields + §5 footprint.

Adds:
- ``lifetime_completions`` — count of MissionInstance rows in
  COMPLETE status for this template.
- ``active_instances`` — list of currently-ACTIVE runs with their
  current node key + contract holder name.

The authoring tool surfaces these so authors can see at a glance
how their template is being consumed.

summary.description: Rich IC opening lore (mission bookend).

base_weight.description: Relative weight in the availability draw.

created_in_era.description: Arc association — the era this mission was authored for.

arc_scope.description:
Whether this is offered globally, per-org, or per-giver.

* `global` - Global

visibility.description:
Audience gate (#870): OPEN surfaces the template to everyone (availability_rule is not consulted); RESTRICTED makes the availability_rule predicate the eligibility gate — an empty rule admits no PC (the emergent staff-only / in-testing state, and the production-safe default for new templates). Staff (is_staff_observer) always bypass.

* `open` - Open
* `restricted` - Restricted

categories.description: Content-type tags for this mission (multi-valued, e.g. assassination, courtly, heist). Drives browse/filter in the authoring tool.

availability_rule.description: Phase 0 predicate tree gating front-door availability for this template.

active_instances.description:
Flatten ACTIVE runs into the response — one row per instance.

SharedMemoryModel identity map keeps current_node + participants
FK-cached after the prefetch; the in-Python walk fires no extra
queries beyond the prefetched ones.

MissionTemplateRequest.description:
List + detail serializer for MissionTemplate browse.

Read-only fields cover the authoring footprint: name, summary,
epilogue, level band, risk tier, weighting, era association, scope,
cooldown, reward-group rule, active flag, visibility, categories,
availability rule.

The visibility flip is a straight write (#870): there is no
"publish to nobody" failure mode to guard against — a RESTRICTED
template whose rule admits no PC simply IS staff-only, a valid
emergent state. ``availability_rule`` IS validated (well-formedness;
a malformed tree would crash every later availability check).

summary.description: Rich IC opening lore (mission bookend).

base_weight.description: Relative weight in the availability draw.

created_in_era.description: Arc association — the era this mission was authored for.

faction_affiliation.description: Optional org this role fronts for (e.g., Builders Guild Clerk → Builders Guild Organization). Used by org-scoped permission filters.

name.description: Display name; e.g., 'Builders Guild Clerk', 'Town Guard'.

label.description: UI display text for the menu option.

draw_mode.description:
MENU = deterministic option always shown if eligible. POOL = NPC draws from a pool per visit (mission-style; #686).

* `menu` - Menu
* `pool` - Pool

eligibility_rule.description: Predicate JSON evaluated via `world.missions.predicates.evaluate`. Empty dict = no gate. Drives both visibility and selectability — if it fails, the offer doesn't appear in the menu.

rapport_requirement.description: Minimum in-interaction rapport to see/select this option. Separate from durable eligibility; 0 = no rapport gate.

is_final.description: Final actions resolve + end the interaction. Non-final actions adjust rapport and re-render the menu (e.g., flattery/negotiation).

rapport_delta_success.description: Rapport delta on success of a non-final check-based action.

rapport_delta_failure.description: Rapport delta on failure of a non-final check-based action.

cooldown.description: Per-(offer, persona) throttle applied after a final-action grant. When set, `OfferCooldown.available_at = now + cooldown` blocks re-selection until it elapses. Null = no cooldown.

check_type.description: Optional: if set, non-final actions roll `perform_check` against this check type and `check_difficulty`. Success → `rapport_delta_success`; failure → `rapport_delta_failure`. Final actions ignore this — the effect IS the payoff.

check_difficulty.description: Target difficulty passed to `perform_check` when `check_type` is set. Meaningless when `check_type` is null.

NPCStanding.description:
Staff CRUD for per-(PC persona, NPC persona) affection rows.

Standing carries affection + interaction summary only — cooldown

persona.description: The PC's persona that holds this standing.

npc_persona.description: The NPC's persona the standing is with.

affection.description: Per-persona-pair standing / affection. Predicate gate `min_npc_standing` reads this. Negative values mean disliked. Movement mechanic (flirt/seduce/cultivation checks) is adjacent gameplay work that mutates this value; the model just carries it.

last_interaction_summary.description: Free-text summary of the last interaction; used by both mission and functionary contexts to surface 'why we left off where we did'.

Standing carries affection + interaction summary only — cooldown

POST body for creating a summons (GM/staff only).

Validates that the offer and target persona exist and that the offer is
MISSION-kind. Object-level validation lives here so the view stays thin
and the serializer is the single authority on input correctness.

offer.description: The offer this summons directs at a target.

Opponent.description:
Read serializer for combat opponents.

Soak value and probing threshold are GM-only — players discover
these through gameplay (probing attacks, combo availability).

active_conditions.description:
Active conditions on this opponent's in-world ObjectDB.

Public conditions (``is_visible_to_others=True``) are shown to
everyone; hidden conditions only to GM/staff. Ordered by
``display_priority`` (highest first). Opponents with no backing
ObjectDB (or none applied) serialize to ``[]``.

thumbnail_url.description:
Direct portrait URL, resolved through the opponent's persona.

Mirrors ``PersonaSerializer.thumbnail_url`` (the persona's
``thumbnail_url`` URLField — ``""`` when unset). Persona-less
opponents (``persona=None``) return ``None``.

thumbnail_media_url.description:
PlayerMedia portrait URL, dynamically resolved (#2196).

Uses ``resolve_thumbnail()`` when the opponent has a persona (character).
For persona-less (generic/ephemeral) NPCs, falls back to the opponent's
own ``portrait`` FK via ``fallback_media``.

OpponentDefaultsResponse.description:
Read-only response serializer for the opponent-defaults preview endpoint.

Contains all ``OpponentStatBlock`` scalar fields + ``phases`` + the two
stakes-gate advisory fields.  Used only for ``@extend_schema`` so that
drf-spectacular emits the correct component instead of inferring the
viewset's default ``EncounterDetail`` schema.

OpponentRequest.description:
Read serializer for combat opponents.

Soak value and probing threshold are GM-only — players discover
these through gameplay (probing attacks, combo availability).

OpportunityRow.description: One discovery row — name, summary, pointer, source flavor.

OrgBooks.description: The whole books page in one read.

description.description: A description of the organization's purpose and history

words.description: Words / motto — house words, gang credo, guild maxim (#2079).

organization.description: The organization this membership belongs to

persona.description: The persona (character identity) that holds this membership

joined_date.description: When the persona joined this organization

left_at.description: When the persona voluntarily left the organization

exiled_at.description: When the persona was forcibly removed from the organization

name.description: Diegetic name for this rung (e.g., Guildmaster, Captain)

can_invite.description: Members at this rank can invite others to the organization

can_kick.description: Members at this rank can expel lower-ranked members

can_manage_ranks.description: Members at this rank can promote/demote others

can_lead_rituals.description: Members at this rank may lead this organization's group rituals. No org-ritual dispatch mechanism consumes this yet for non-Covenant organizations — see needs-design follow-up filed alongside #708 ('Generic organization-ritual dispatch for non-Covenant org kinds'). Mirrors CovenantRank.can_lead_rituals, which IS consumed today by Covenant Sanctification.

OrganizationReputation.description: A persona's standing with an organization — named tier only, never the raw value.

persona.description: The persona (character identity) this reputation belongs to

organization.description: The organization this reputation is with

OutfitRead.description: Read serializer for Outfit — nests slot rows.

character_sheet.description: The character this sheet belongs to

wardrobe.description: The wardrobe ItemInstance this outfit is stored in.

OutfitRenameRequest.description:
Write serializer for renames/redescribes (PUT / PATCH on Outfit).

Distinct from ``OutfitWriteSerializer`` because update only touches
``name`` and ``description`` — exposing ``character_sheet`` and
``wardrobe`` in the request schema would imply they're editable when
they're write-once. The viewset wires this serializer via
``@extend_schema`` on update/partial_update so the public API contract
matches reality.

OutfitSlotRead.description: Read serializer for OutfitSlot — nests the item instance.

OutfitSlotWriteRequest.description:
Write serializer for OutfitSlot — validates POST input for AddOutfitSlotAction.

``OutfitSlotViewSet.create`` calls ``.is_valid()`` on this serializer and then
dispatches through ``AddOutfitSlotAction`` directly (which wraps the
``add_outfit_slot`` service) rather than calling ``.save()`` — so this
serializer has no ``create()`` override; it's validation-only.

OutfitWriteRequest.description:
Write serializer for Outfit — validates POST input for SaveOutfitAction.

``OutfitViewSet.create`` calls ``.is_valid()`` on this serializer and then
dispatches through ``SaveOutfitAction`` directly (which wraps the
``save_outfit`` service) rather than calling ``.save()`` — so this
serializer has no ``create()``/``update()`` override; it's validation-only.

character_sheet.description: The character this sheet belongs to

wardrobe.description: The wardrobe ItemInstance this outfit is stored in.

Participant.description:
Read serializer for combat participants.

Vitals (health, max_health, character_status) are private by default.
Only visible to staff, the scene GM, or the player who owns the
character — same visibility rules as character sheets.

character_sheet_id.description: The character this sheet belongs to

health.description: Return current health — only if viewer has permission.

max_health.description: Return max health — only if viewer has permission.

character_status.description:
Return a coarse, read-only life status — only if viewer has permission.

Derived at read time from life_state + active conditions + agency (there
is no persisted status field anymore). The richer frontend status surface
is tracked by #521/#522.

available_strain.description:
Return strain budget (anima pool) — only if viewer owns the PC.

Uses the same vitals-visibility rules as health/status fields.
The frontend's YourTurn strain slider reads this as its max value.

fatigue.description:
Return the three fatigue pools (physical/social/mental).

Each pool is ``{"current": N, "capacity": M}``. ``current`` reads the
persisted ``FatiguePool`` row (0 when no row exists yet); ``capacity``
is derived from the character's endurance stats via
``get_fatigue_capacity``. Gated by the same vitals-visibility rules as
health/strain — outsiders get ``None``.

The frontend's VitalPools component reads this to render fatigue bars
(replacing the ``0/10`` placeholder). See #552.

active_conditions.description:
Active conditions on this participant's character ObjectDB.

Public conditions (``is_visible_to_others=True``) are shown to
everyone; hidden conditions only to the character's owner, GMs, or
staff — reusing the same ownership gate as vitals
(``_can_view_vitals``). Ordered by ``display_priority`` (highest
first). No conditions → ``[]``.

thumbnail_url.description:
Direct portrait URL via the primary persona (mirrors OpponentSerializer).

The persona's ``thumbnail_url`` URLField (``""`` when unset); ``None``
when the character has no primary persona.

thumbnail_media_url.description:
PlayerMedia portrait URL, dynamically resolved (#2196).

Uses ``resolve_thumbnail()`` via the primary persona's character.
``None`` when there is no primary persona.

escalation_level.description: Escalation pressure on this combatant — public dramatic state.

intensity_modifier.description: Process-derived intensity bonus from the COMBAT engagement.

control_modifier.description: Process-derived control bonus from the COMBAT engagement.

ParticipantRequest.description:
Read serializer for combat participants.

Vitals (health, max_health, character_status) are private by default.
Only visible to staff, the scene GM, or the player who owns the
character — same visibility rules as character sheets.

ParticipationRuleEnum.description:
* `SINGLE_ACTOR` - Single Actor
* `FORMATION` - Formation (all must accept, ≥2)
* `INDUCTION` - Induction (majority of respondents)
* `BILATERAL` - Bilateral (exactly 2, both must accept)

PatchedBeatRequest.description: Full serializer for Beat including all Phase 2 predicate config fields.

outcome.description:
The story's current outcome on this beat — a single shared value across the story's progression (the owning character for CHARACTER scope, the group for GROUP scope, the world for GLOBAL scope). A story has exactly one progression trail, so this field represents the whole story's state, not per-character state. Historical per-character contributions live in BeatCompletion.

* `unsatisfied` - Unsatisfied
* `success` - Success
* `failure` - Failure
* `expired` - Expired
* `pending_gm_review` - Pending GM review

internal_description.description: Author/Lead GM/staff view: real predicate + meaning.

player_hint.description: Shown while active (if visibility=HINTED or VISIBLE).

player_resolution_text.description: Shown in story log after beat completes.

advances.description: False = Tangent: recorded for history, never gates a transition.

risk.description:
Stakes declaration — how life-threatening/consequential this beat is. Drives Legend award magnitude on SUCCESS (see world.societies.constants.RISK_LEGEND_AWARDS). Authoring trust-gated in the serializer.

* `none` - None
* `low` - Low
* `moderate` - Moderate
* `high` - High
* `extreme` - Extreme

target_level.description: The character level this beat's stakes are declared against ('EXTREME at level 4'). Effective risk at activation is computed from the gap between this and the actual party's average level. Required (via readiness validation, not clean()) when risk != NONE.

referenced_milestone_type.description:
Which kind of milestone to check on referenced_story.

* `story_resolved` - Story resolved
* `chapter_reached` - Chapter reached or passed
* `episode_reached` - Episode reached or passed

required_points.description: For AGGREGATE_THRESHOLD predicates — total contribution points required.

agm_eligible.description: Lead GM may flag this beat to be claimable by Assistant GMs.

deadline.description: Optional wall-clock deadline. Expiry handling deferred to Phase 3+.

success_consequences.description: ConsequencePool to fire when this beat resolves SUCCESS.

failure_consequences.description: ConsequencePool to fire when this beat resolves FAILURE.

expired_consequences.description: ConsequencePool to fire when this beat resolves EXPIRED.

required_mission.description: Optional: a MissionTemplate this beat requires (Phase 5b.3 data shape only; engine deferred). SET_NULL on template delete.

description.description: What the player observed.

PatchedCatalogSuggestionDetailRequest.description:
For staff triaging GM scenario-catalog suggestions (#2127).

No create serializer — creation only happens through
``SubmitCatalogSuggestionAction`` (the generic REGISTRY dispatch seam both
telnet and web use), never a direct DRF POST.

summary.description: Summary of what happened in this chapter

consequences.description: Key consequences that affect future chapters

character.description: The character this anima belongs to.
The character this aura belongs to.

current_stage.description:
Current stage in character creation flow

* `1` - Origin
* `2` - Heritage
* `3` - Lineage
* `4` - Distinctions
* `5` - Path & Skills
* `6` - Attributes
* `7` - Magic
* `8` - Appearance
* `9` - Identity
* `10` - Final Touches
* `11` - Review

draft_data.description: Staged data: stats, skills, traits, identity, etc.

character.description: The character who knows this gift.

character_sheet.description: The character sheet this resonance is attached to.

lifetime_earned.description: Monotonic total of resonance earned (never decremented).

flavor_text.description: Optional player-defined description of how this resonance manifests.

Serializer for CovenantRank (the per-covenant authority ladder).

Read: exposes all rank fields.
Write: validates tier uniqueness per covenant and capability flags.

description.description: Optional flavor/ceremony text.

can_kick.description: May remove members of a strictly lower tier.

can_manage_ranks.description: May edit the ladder and assign members.

can_lead_rituals.description: May lead this covenant's group rituals (e.g. Covenant Sanctification).

can_request_gm.description: May post an open ask for a GM to run a story for this covenant (GroupStoryRequest, #2119). Distinct from can_invite — petitioning an outside GM commits the covenant to outside oversight.

PatchedEncounterDetailRequest.description: Full encounter state with covenant-filtered action visibility.

pace_timer_minutes.description: Minutes before auto-resolving in timed mode.

round_started_at.description: When the current declaration phase began.

summary.description: Summary of this episode's plot beats

resting_conclusion.description: Player-facing text shown when progress RESTS at this episode (no chosen transition). Required before PLOT promotion.

is_ending.description: Explicit 'this is an ending' marker; satisfies PLOT promotion when there is no outbound transition.

consequences.description: What consequences lead to the next episode

Full serializer for EpisodeProgressionRequirement.

Records a beat that must reach ``required_outcome`` before any outbound
transition fires from the episode.

PatchedEraRequest.description: Full serializer for Era — includes read-only story_count context field.

PatchedEventUpdateRequest.description: Serializer for updating events. Only mutable fields are writable.
