scheduled_real_time.description: The OOC date/time players schedule for

scheduled_ic_time.description: IC datetime — derived from game clock, then adjustable

time_phase.description:
Time-of-day phase for the event (scene time freezes here)

* `dawn` - Dawn
* `day` - Day
* `dusk` - Dusk
* `night` - Night

application_text.description: Freeform: what the applicant wants to GM, who they'd run for, what stories they'd tell.

PatchedGMTableMembershipRequest.description: Serializer for persona memberships at GM tables.

PatchedGMTableRequest.description:
Serializer for GM tables.

Computed read-only fields:
- member_count: active memberships (left_at__isnull=True)
- story_count: stories with primary_table=this table
- viewer_role: "gm" / "staff" / "member" / "guest" / "none" derived from
  request.user vs. table.gm.account and membership/participation lookups

description.description: Player-facing description of this gift.

PatchedGlobalStoryProgressRequest.description: Serializer for GlobalStoryProgress — singleton metaplot progress pointer.

current_episode.description: Null while the story is at the frontier or before start.

PatchedGroupStoryProgressRequest.description: Serializer for GroupStoryProgress — per-GMTable progress pointer.

current_episode.description: Null while the story is at the frontier (unauthored) or before start.

PatchedManageTrainingUpdateRequest.description: Validate input for updating a training allocation.

PatchedMissionGiverRequest.description:
Staff CRUD for trigger-based MissionGiver rows (#729).

Covers the two surviving GiverKind variants (ROOM_TRIGGER,
ENVIRONMENTAL_DETAIL). ``templates`` is a flat M2M draw pool — each
template self-gates at draw time via its own ``availability_rule``
(Option A), so there are no per-attachment overrides here.

giver_kind.description:
How this giver reaches the player; selects the target's expected typeclass. ROOM_TRIGGER → Room; ENVIRONMENTAL_DETAIL → examinable item (auto-grants one); BOARD → examinable item that lists many postings (preview-then-take, #2044).

* `environmental_detail` - Environmental Detail
* `room_trigger` - Room Trigger
* `board` - Notice Board

target.description: The Evennia object this giver is bound to. Its typeclass must match giver_kind: NPC → Character-typeclass; ROOM_TRIGGER → Room-typeclass; ENVIRONMENTAL_DETAIL → any non-Character/Room/Exit Object (an examinable item or room detail). Null = draft (see is_publishable). All FK targets land in ObjectDB; the kind enum + clean() typeclass check enforce semantic shape without the wasted nullable columns of a discriminator.

org.description: Optional organization this giver fronts for (used by ORG arc-scope).

templates.description: The mission templates this trigger giver offers (#729). A flat draw pool — each template self-gates at draw time via its own availability_rule, so per-attachment overrides aren't needed (Option A).

PatchedMissionNodeRequest.description:
Editor CRUD for MissionNode rows.

``allowed_riders`` exposes the consequence M2M as a list of PKs (the
authoring UI passes them through unchanged). Editor layout fields
(editor_x / editor_y) round-trip; flavor_text and its needs_rewrite
sibling are both editable. ``location_mode``/``locations``/``target_area``
round-trip the node's location gate (#885, #888).

key.description: Stable per-template node key (unique within the template).

conflict_mode.description:
How contested option choices resolve for multiple participants.

* `group_vote` - Group Vote
* `joint` - Joint

joint_combine.description:
JOINT mode: how participant results combine.

* `any` - Any
* `all` - All
* `count` - Count

joint_count.description: JOINT+COUNT mode: minimum number of successes required.

allowed_riders.description: Reusable consequence riders permitted at this node. NOT consumed by the engine in Phase A; reserved for future use.

deny_all_riders.description: When true, no consequence riders may attach at this node. NOT consumed by the engine in Phase A; reserved for future use.

editor_x.description: Mission Studio (Phase E) canvas X coordinate. Pure authoring metadata — no engine meaning. IntegerField (negatives allowed) so authors can pan to negative coords.

editor_y.description: Mission Studio (Phase E) canvas Y coordinate. Pure authoring metadata — no engine meaning. IntegerField (negatives allowed) so authors can pan to negative coords.

flavor_text.description: Thin abstract description of the moment shown to the player when they enter this node (design §8.2). Paragraph-style (TextField, unbounded) — the short per-option label is the option's authored_ic_framing (CharField/200). The rich narration is the player-authored Legend Entry; this is just the engine's framing line.

flavor_text_needs_rewrite.description: Phase-D copy service sets True (inherited copy reads as 'rewrite me'); the Phase-D edit service clears it on save. Surfaces in the Studio's 'N flavor fields are still flagged as un-rewritten copy' counter (design §10). NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service responsibility.

location_mode.description:
Default location gate for this node's options (#885): ANYWHERE = live wherever the character is; ANCHOR = live only in the instance's grant-time anchor room; ROOMS = live in this node's authored ``locations`` set. An option with its own ``locations`` rows overrides this default.

* `anywhere` - Anywhere
* `anchor` - Anchor Room
* `rooms` - Authored Rooms
* `instance` - Spawned Instance
* `area` - Target Area

locations.description: Authored rooms where this node's options are live (consulted only when ``location_mode=ROOMS``). Options may override per-option via ``MissionOption.locations``.

target_area.description: Target area for AREA location_mode. A room matches when its RoomProfile.area is this area or any descendant via AreaClosure.

PatchedMissionOfferDetailsRequest.description:
Staff CRUD for mission-kind offer details (#728).

``role`` is denormalized from ``offer.role`` by the model's ``save()``
override (per #686 Phase 6) so callers never set it directly — it's
read-only here, and the unique-constraint promise on
``(role, mission_template)`` is enforced at the DB level.

source_beat.description: Optional: the staked Beat this offer resolves. When set, accepting the offer copies it onto MissionInstance.source_beat, arming the #1770-PR4 stakes gate + contract activation. Independent of Beat.required_mission (ADR-0010). SET_NULL on Beat delete.

target_project.description: Optional: the live Project this offer's missions advance (#2045). When set, accepting the offer copies it onto MissionInstance.target_project at issuance — exactly how source_beat works. SET_NULL on Project delete (a cancelled project unbinds the offer; issuing then refuses).

weight.description: Per-offer weight override for POOL draw. Null falls back to MissionTemplate.base_weight.

requirements_override.description: Predicate JSON AND-composed with MissionTemplate.availability_rule and NPCServiceOffer.eligibility_rule at evaluation time. Empty dict = no additional gate.

role_cooldown_duration.description: Duration written into NPCRoleCooldown on accept (blocks OTHER missions on the role for this persona). Null falls back to MissionTemplate.cooldown.

draw_priority.description: POOL-draw priority tier (#726). The highest tier present is drawn first with guaranteed inclusion (up to the pool count); lower tiers fill any remaining slots via the weighted draw. Give chain-unlock / high-stakes follow-up missions a positive value so they surface ahead of the general pool. 0 = general pool.

PatchedMissionOptionRequest.description:
Editor CRUD for MissionOption rows (authored or challenge-sourced).

Both source_kind values are editable; consumer code distinguishes via
the kind field (BRANCH vs CHECK). visibility_rule is a JSONField that
rides through; the predicate-tree builder API (D5) is what authors
actually use to write it.

order.description: Display/evaluation order within the node (no Meta.ordering — callers order explicitly).

visibility_rule.description: Phase 0 predicate tree gating this option's visibility.

authored_check_type.description: AUTHORED+CHECK: the check resolved by this option.

authored_ic_framing.description: Short option-row label shown in the choice list (CharField/200). Keep terse; the longer 'what happens at this node' narration belongs in MissionNode.flavor_text.

authored_ic_framing_needs_rewrite.description: Phase-D copy service sets True (inherited copy reads as 'rewrite me'); the Phase-D edit service clears it on save. Surfaces in the Studio's 'N flavor fields are still flagged as un-rewritten copy' counter (design §10). NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service responsibility.

branch_target.description: BRANCH/authored route: the node this option leads to.

challenge.description: CHALLENGE source: the challenge whose approaches fan out into this option's challenge-contributed options at runtime. on_delete=PROTECT — detach all referencing options before deleting the challenge.

consequence.description: Optional per-candidate consequence override; falls back to the parent route's consequence when null. STORED BUT UNCONSUMED in Phase B — Phase D wires per-candidate emission.

outcome_text.description: Optional per-candidate outcome text shown to the player. STORED BUT UNCONSUMED in Phase B — Phase D wires it.

outcome_text_needs_rewrite.description: Phase-D copy service sets True; the Phase-D edit service clears it on save. NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service responsibility.

PatchedMissionOptionRouteRequest.description: Editor CRUD for MissionOptionRoute rows (one per outcome tier per option).

outcome_tier.description: Resolved outcome tier; null = the single BRANCH route.

is_random_set.description: When true, destination is drawn from weighted candidates.

consequence.description: Authored structured effect applied when this route's outcome tier is rolled; null = pure routing/no effect.

outcome_text.description: Player-facing outcome text shown when this route's tier is rolled (design §8.3). WIRED by #885 — the resolution engine surfaces it as the actor's STORY message (a random-set route prefers the chosen candidate's outcome_text, #941).

outcome_text_needs_rewrite.description: The copy service sets True on cloned text; the editor serializer clears it when the text is rewritten (#941). NOT cleared automatically at the model layer — service/serializer responsibility.

PatchedMissionOptionRouteRewardRequest.description:
Editor CRUD for reward lines attached to a route OR a candidate (XOR).

Model-level CheckConstraint + clean() both enforce exactly-one-parent.
DRF's ModelSerializer skips clean() by default, so we mirror the XOR
check here as ``validate()`` to surface a clean 400 instead of letting
the DB constraint raise IntegrityError → 500.

route.description: Parent route (route-level reward). Exactly one of route / candidate must be set; enforced in clean().

candidate.description: Parent candidate (per-candidate reward bundle — design §8.3). STORED BUT UNCONSUMED in Phase B; Phase D wires emission when a random candidate fires. Exactly one of route / candidate must be set.

kind.description:
When the emitted line pays out (IMMEDIATE/POST_CRON/PROPAGATION).

* `immediate` - Immediate
* `post_cron` - Post Cron
* `propagation` - Propagation

sink.description:
Which ledger the emitted line pays into.

* `money` - Money
* `legend_points` - Legend Points
* `resonance` - Resonance
* `rumor` - Rumor
* `crime_watch` - Crime Watch
* `beat` - Beat
* `item` - Item
* `summons` - Follow-On Summons
* `project` - Project

amount.description: Numeric magnitude of the broadcast reward, when applicable.

followon_offer.description: Required when sink=FOLLOW_ON_SUMMONS: the MISSION-kind offer whose summons fires as the follow-on reward.

followon_message.description: IC text for the follow-on summons when sink=FOLLOW_ON_SUMMONS. Empty = the summons shows the offer's default text.

followon_expiry_hours.description: When sink=FOLLOW_ON_SUMMONS: the summons lapses after this many hours if unanswered (triggering the refusal/escalation path). Null = no expiry (the summons persists until accepted or declined).

contract_holder_only.description: True → emit exactly one line to the instance's contract holder, regardless of how many participants ran the mission. False → distribute per the template's reward_group_rule.

PatchedMissionTemplateRequest.description:
List + detail serializer for MissionTemplate browse.

Read-only fields cover the authoring footprint: name, summary,
epilogue, level band, risk tier, weighting, era association, scope,
cooldown, reward-group rule, active flag, visibility, categories,
availability rule.

The visibility flip is a straight write (#870): there is no
"publish to nobody" failure mode to guard against — a RESTRICTED
template whose rule admits no PC simply IS staff-only, a valid
emergent state. ``availability_rule`` IS validated (well-formedness;
a malformed tree would crash every later availability check).

summary.description: Rich IC opening lore (mission bookend).

base_weight.description: Relative weight in the availability draw.

created_in_era.description: Arc association — the era this mission was authored for.

arc_scope.description:
Whether this is offered globally, per-org, or per-giver.

* `global` - Global
* `org` - Organization
* `giver` - Giver

percent_replace.description: Percent chance this template replaces an existing offer (0-100).

cooldown.description: Per-giver re-offer cooldown. Must be non-negative.

reward_group_rule.description:
Multi-participant payout split (authoring knob only; actual distribution-by-rule is Phase 5).

* `all_equal` - All Equal
* `by_role` - By Role
* `by_participation` - By Participation

visibility.description:
Audience gate (#870): OPEN surfaces the template to everyone (availability_rule is not consulted); RESTRICTED makes the availability_rule predicate the eligibility gate — an empty rule admits no PC (the emergent staff-only / in-testing state, and the production-safe default for new templates). Staff (is_staff_observer) always bypass.

* `open` - Open
* `restricted` - Restricted

categories.description: Content-type tags for this mission (multi-valued, e.g. assassination, courtly, heist). Drives browse/filter in the authoring tool.

availability_rule.description: Phase 0 predicate tree gating front-door availability for this template.

name.description: Display name; e.g., 'Builders Guild Clerk', 'Town Guard'.

description.description: Admin-editable flavor; what this role does in the world.

default_description_template.description: Fallback flavor description rendered when a class-1 nameless NPC of this role is presented to the player. Class-2+ NPCs use their persona's description instead.

default_rapport_starting_value.description: Initial in-interaction rapport for class-1 interactions. Class-2/3/4 interactions start at `default + NPCStanding.affection`.

faction_affiliation.description: Optional org this role fronts for (e.g., Builders Guild Clerk → Builders Guild Organization). Used by org-scoped permission filters.

is_active.description: Master on/off switch for the role (#686). When False, no offer on this role is eligible in available_offers, regardless of per-offer state. Staff use this to disable a role without deleting it; migrated value from the legacy `MissionGiver.is_active`.

kind.description:
Discriminator: routes to per-kind details model + effect handler registered in `world.npc_services.effects.OFFER_EFFECT_HANDLERS`.

* `permit` - Permit
* `mission` - Mission
* `loan` - Loan
* `collection` - Collection
* `improvement` - Improvement
* `court_grant` - Court Grant
* `informant` - Informant
* `contact` - Contact
* `personal_favor` - Personal Favor
* `guard` - Guard
* `fan` - Fan
* `minor_ally` - Minor Ally

label.description: UI display text for the menu option.

draw_mode.description:
MENU = deterministic option always shown if eligible. POOL = NPC draws from a pool per visit (mission-style; #686).

* `menu` - Menu
* `pool` - Pool

eligibility_rule.description: Predicate JSON evaluated via `world.missions.predicates.evaluate`. Empty dict = no gate. Drives both visibility and selectability — if it fails, the offer doesn't appear in the menu.

rapport_requirement.description: Minimum in-interaction rapport to see/select this option. Separate from durable eligibility; 0 = no rapport gate.

is_final.description: Final actions resolve + end the interaction. Non-final actions adjust rapport and re-render the menu (e.g., flattery/negotiation).

rapport_delta_success.description: Rapport delta on success of a non-final check-based action.

rapport_delta_failure.description: Rapport delta on failure of a non-final check-based action.

cooldown.description: Per-(offer, persona) throttle applied after a final-action grant. When set, `OfferCooldown.available_at = now + cooldown` blocks re-selection until it elapses. Null = no cooldown.

check_type.description: Optional: if set, non-final actions roll `perform_check` against this check type and `check_difficulty`. Success → `rapport_delta_success`; failure → `rapport_delta_failure`. Final actions ignore this — the effect IS the payoff.

check_difficulty.description: Target difficulty passed to `perform_check` when `check_type` is set. Meaningless when `check_type` is null.

PatchedNPCStandingRequest.description:
Staff CRUD for per-(PC persona, NPC persona) affection rows.

Standing carries affection + interaction summary only — cooldown
lives on OfferCooldown so it works for every offer kind.

persona.description: The PC's persona that holds this standing.

npc_persona.description: The NPC's persona the standing is with.

affection.description: Per-persona-pair standing / affection. Predicate gate `min_npc_standing` reads this. Negative values mean disliked. Movement mechanic (flirt/seduce/cultivation checks) is adjacent gameplay work that mutates this value; the model just carries it.

last_interaction_summary.description: Free-text summary of the last interaction; used by both mission and functionary contexts to surface 'why we left off where we did'.

PatchedOfferCooldownRequest.description:
Staff CRUD for per-(offer, persona) cooldown rows.

Written by `resolve_offer` on final-action grants; staff can clear
or extend by editing `available_at` directly.

available_at.description: Earliest time `persona` can select this offer again.

offer.description: The offer this summons directs at a target.

target_persona.description: The persona this summons is directed at.

message.description: IC text — what the servant learns of the master's wish.

expires_at.description: When this summons lapses if unanswered. Null = no expiry.

created_by.description: The GM who created this summons. Null for staff-created.

PatchedOutfitRenameRequest.description:
Write serializer for renames/redescribes (PUT / PATCH on Outfit).

Distinct from ``OutfitWriteSerializer`` because update only touches
``name`` and ``description`` — exposing ``character_sheet`` and
``wardrobe`` in the request schema would imply they're editable when
they're write-once. The viewset wires this serializer via
``@extend_schema`` on update/partial_update so the public API contract
matches reality.

PatchedPermitOfferDetailsRequest.description:
Staff CRUD for permit-kind offer details (#1684, closing #728's residual).

``building_kind`` and ``default_approved_wards`` are PK-related writes; the
FE feeds them from the existing ``/api/buildings/building-kinds/`` and
areas list endpoints.

offer.description: The NPCServiceOffer row this details model decorates.

building_kind.description: Which BuildingKind this offer issues permits for. Required for kind=PERMIT offers in Plan 3 (nullable in the schema so existing rows can migrate; runtime issuance asserts non-null).

default_approved_wards.description: Default set of wards the issued permit is valid in. Snapshotted onto BuildingPermitDetails.approved_wards at issuance time.

default_max_target_size.description: Default cap on ``target_size`` for buildings constructed under permits from this offer.

permit_cost_currency.description: Currency cost of the permit (approval fee — distinct from construction cost). Charged to the PC's account at grant time.

PatchedPlayerBoundaryRequest.description:
Owner-authored ``PlayerBoundary`` CRUD shape.

``owner`` is never client-writable — the viewset sets it from the
requesting player's own ``PlayerData`` on create, so a player can never
author a boundary for anyone else. Mounted behind an owner-scoped
queryset (see ``PlayerBoundaryViewSet.get_queryset``), so this serializer
is only ever handed rows the requester already owns; there is no
non-owner read path through this class.

theme.description: Required for HARD_LINE (the machine-matched category); optional for ADVISORY.

detail.description: Free-text nuance. For HARD_LINE this is staff/audit-only and never surfaced.

visibility_mode.description:
Who can see this content.

* `public` - Public
* `private` - Private
* `characters` - Specific Characters
* `groups` - Consent Groups

visible_to_tenures.description: Tenures who can see this (if mode is 'characters').

visible_to_groups.description: Consent groups who can see this (if mode is 'groups').

excluded_tenures.description: Tenures explicitly excluded even if otherwise visible.

reporter_persona.description: The persona the submitter was wearing when they submitted.

description.description: Freeform feedback text from the player.

location.description: Room the submitter was in at the time, if any.

PatchedPlayerReportDetailRequest.description: Staff-only detail serializer with full identity context.

reporter_persona.description: The persona the reporter was wearing when submitting.

reported_persona.description: The persona of the reported player at the time of the incident.

category.description:
Report category (#1279) — placeholder set, TBD.

* `harassment` - Harassment
* `ooc_abuse` - OOC Abuse
* `red_flag` - FYI / Red Flag
* `other` - Other

behavior_description.description: What the reported persona did. Stub — wording needs design pass.

asked_to_stop.description: Whether the reporter asked the other player to stop the behavior.

blocked_or_muted.description: Whether the reporter blocked or muted as a result.

scene.description: The scene where the behavior occurred, if applicable.

interaction.description: A specific flagged interaction, if applicable.

location.description: Room the reporter was in when submitting.

PatchedRiskCalibrationRequest.description:
Full serializer for RiskCalibration (#1770 pillar 5).

Staff-write / authenticated-read — enforced by IsStaffOrReadOnly on the
ViewSet, not here.

PatchedRitualPatchRequest.description:
Write serializer for partial PATCH of player-authored Rituals.

Handles top-level Ritual fields (name, description, narrative_prose) and
optional nested ``check_config`` when the ritual has one. Rituals without a
config silently ignore check_config if supplied.

privacy_mode.description:
Privacy floor for all interactions in this scene

* `public` - Public
* `private` - Private
* `ephemeral` - Ephemeral

owner_tenure.description: Tenure that owns the preference (does NOT want these actions).

blocked_tenure.description: Tenure barred from targeting owner_tenure in this category.

mode.description:
EVERYONE (anyone), ALL_BUT_BLACKLIST (anyone but this category's blacklist), FRIENDS_WHITELIST (OOC friends + whitelist), or ALLOWLIST (only whitelisted actors).

* `everyone` - Everyone
* `all_but_blacklist` - Everyone except my blacklist
* `friends_whitelist` - Friends and my whitelist
* `rivals` - My declared rivals (and whitelist)
* `allowlist` - Allowlist only

PatchedSocialConsentPreferenceRequest.description: Serializer for per-tenure social consent preferences.

allow_social_actions.description: If False, this character never appears as a valid social action target.

owner_tenure.description: Tenure that owns the preference (receives social actions).

allowed_tenure.description: Tenure permitted to target owner_tenure with social actions.

PatchedStakeRequest.description:
Full serializer for Stake (#1770 pillar 1).

Template-set path denormalizes subject_kind/severity from the template
(so a later template retune never rewrites live contracts) and validates
the beat's declared risk falls within the template's [min_risk, max_risk]
band (by risk_index). The template-null (CUSTOM) path is gated to staff or
a non-staff GM whose GMLevelCap.allow_custom_stakes is set (see
`_gm_allows_custom_stakes`), mirroring BeatSerializer.validate's risk gate
in style. Any write (create or update) is rejected while the beat carries
an open StakeContractActivation — the lock (#1770 pillar 8).
``outcomes`` (PR2) exposes the read-only resolution audit rows.

template.description: Null only for trust-gated custom stakes.

subject_sheet.description: For NPC_FATE / PERSONAL_JEOPARDY subjects. Nulls if the sheet is deleted.

subject_item.description: For ITEM subjects. Nulls if the item instance is deleted/consumed.

subject_society.description: For FACTION subjects (society-level). Nulls if the society is deleted.

subject_organization.description: For FACTION subjects (organization-level). Nulls if the org is deleted.

subject_label.description: Freeform subject name (CUSTOM / CAMPAIGN_TRACK, or flavor).

player_summary.description: Player-facing line shown at opt-in: what is wagered, how badly.

PatchedStakeResolutionRequest.description:
Full serializer for StakeResolution (#1770 pillar 1).

PR2 adds the writer-payload fields and the pillar-12 no-fiat guard:
sets_subject_lifecycle is only legal for NPC_FATE stakes whose subject
sheet is not player-held; item forfeiture and affection deltas must match
the stake's subject kind. No column-ordering/escalation validation (the
fuse walk measures reachability, not monotonicity).
``reward_lines`` (PR3) exposes the branch's authored win-reward payouts
read-only; they are written via the stake-reward-lines endpoint.

outcome_key.description: Short designer-authored slug naming this branch within its column's polarity (#1760) — e.g. 'destroyed', 'captured', 'given_to_allies'. Blank = the column's single default branch (backward compatible with pre-#1760 content). column stays the coarse WIN/LOSS/WITHDRAWAL polarity every severity/reward/machine-grading rule keys off; outcome_key is a finer dimension within it, not a replacement axis.

consequence_pool.description: Pool to fire when this column resolves (tier-aware).

escalates_to_risk.description:
The risk tier the situation spawned by this branch carries — the fuse mechanic. Blank = no escalation declared.

* `none` - None
* `low` - Low
* `moderate` - Moderate
* `high` - High
* `extreme` - Extreme

narrative_summary.description: What happens in the story when this branch fires (GM-authored).

forfeits_subject_item.description: On fire, soft-forfeit the stake's subject_item (ITEM stakes only).

subject_standing_delta.description: On fire, adjust standing between the stake's subject and each participant persona (#1760). NPC_FATE: adjusts NPCStanding via subject_sheet's primary persona (unchanged pre-#1760 behavior). FACTION: adjusts SocietyReputation or OrganizationReputation (whichever of subject_society/subject_organization is set) — previously a dead FK (subject_society/subject_organization were never read); this is the fix.

sets_subject_lifecycle.description:
On fire, set_lifecycle_state(subject_sheet, value). NPC_FATE only, and only when the subject sheet is not player-held (pillar 12).

* `ALIVE` - Alive
* `CAPTURED` - Captured / Unknown
* `COMA` - Coma
* `RETIRED` - Retired
* `DEAD` - Dead

machine_match_lifecycle_state.description:
On automatic (machine) grading, if the stake's subject_sheet's actual lifecycle_state equals this value, THIS branch is selected over the column's plain default (#1760 — generalizes the old is-dead-only override to the full LifecycleState ladder: ALIVE/CAPTURED/COMA/RETIRED/DEAD). NPC_FATE stakes only — blank means no machine-match, resolve via the plain column default or a GM's Constrained Pick.

* `ALIVE` - Alive
* `CAPTURED` - Captured / Unknown
* `COMA` - Coma
* `RETIRED` - Retired
* `DEAD` - Dead

PatchedStakeRewardLineRequest.description:
Full serializer for StakeRewardLine (#1770 PR3 — the contract's win side).

Mirrors StakeResolutionSerializer's gates one hop deeper: the ownership
walk via resolution.stake.beat (create-path enforcement), the two-sided
open-activation lock, the completed-beat refusal, the WIN-column-only
rule, and the sink/resonance shape rule (resonance required iff
sink=RESONANCE; amount >= 1 rides the model validator).
Banding against the tier's reward floor/ceiling is deliberately NOT
rejected here — out-of-band rewards make the contract UNREADY instead
(pillar 7 auto-downgrade); the payout re-checks the band at pay time.

amount.description: Money-equivalent scalar paid to EACH participant (banded by calibration).

resonance.description: Required when sink=RESONANCE; must be null otherwise.

PatchedStakeTemplateRequest.description:
Full serializer for StakeTemplate (#1770 pillar 5, menu-first catalog).

Staff-write / authenticated-read — enforced by IsStaffOrReadOnly on the
ViewSet, not here.

player_summary_template.description: Player-facing summary shown at opt-in; GM fills the specifics.

PatchedStoryDetailRequest.description: Full serializer for story detail views

summary.description: Player-facing 'The Story So Far' — GM-maintained running recap of what has happened and what may lie ahead. Surfaced to players via the role-gated story log, maturity-gated. NOT auto-generated.

scope.description:
Whether this story belongs to one character (CHARACTER), a covenant/group (GROUP), or the whole metaplot (GLOBAL).

* `unassigned` - Unassigned
* `character` - Personal
* `group` - Group
* `global` - Global

impact_tier.description:
Story-side canon-impact tier (#2003). TABLE is never reviewed; REGIONAL auto-clears for EXPERIENCED+ GMs; WORLD requires staff sign-off before staked beats pay (auto-downgrade, never hard-block).

* `table` - Table
* `regional` - Regional
* `world` - World

covenant.description: For GROUP-scope stories: the covenant this storyline belongs to. Informational — not a credit gate. SET_NULL on covenant delete so an archived covenant doesn't cascade-delete its stories.

is_gm_feedback.description: True if feedback is about GM performance

trusted_by_owner.description: Story owner has explicitly trusted this player for this story

PatchedStoryProtectedSubjectRequest.description:
Full serializer for StoryProtectedSubject (#2001 Task 6).

Enforces the model's exactly-one-subject invariant here (DRF serializers
never call ``Model.clean()``) and the story-ownership gate on create/update
— ``IsProtectedSubjectStoryOwnerOrStaff.has_object_permission`` alone
cannot cover create, since DRF never calls object-level permissions for a
row that does not exist yet.

subject_sheet.description: For NPC_FATE / PERSONAL_JEOPARDY subjects. Nulls if the sheet is deleted.

subject_item.description: For ITEM subjects. Nulls if the item instance is deleted/consumed.

subject_society.description: For FACTION subjects (society-level). Nulls if the society is deleted.

subject_organization.description: For FACTION subjects (organization-level). Nulls if the org is deleted.

subject_label.description: Freeform subject name (CUSTOM / CAMPAIGN_TRACK, or a LOCATION fallback).

beat.description: For beat-level refinement: protection applies only while this beat is unsatisfied. Null = story-level (whole arc). SET_NULL (not CASCADE) — a deleted beat must not silently erase the protection; it degrades to story-level instead.

notes.description: GM notes on why this subject is critical. GM-only; never serialized to outsiders.

PatchedSystemErrorReportDetailRequest.description:
Staff read + status-update view of an auto-captured error (#1164).

Everything is read-only except ``status`` — the report is system-authored, so staff
only triage it (OPEN -> REVIEWED / DISMISSED), never edit its captured contents. The
same serializer drives retrieve and update, matching the BugReport pattern.

PatchedTechniqueRequest.description: Serializer for Technique records with intensity and control stats.

name.description: Name of the technique (not unique - different characters can have same name).

gift.description: The gift this technique belongs to.

style.description: The style of this technique (restricted by Path).

effect_type.description: The type of effect this technique produces.

level.description: The level of this technique (determines tier).

intensity.description: Base power of the technique. Determines damage and effect strength.

control.description: Base safety/precision. When intensity exceeds control at runtime, effects become unpredictable and anima cost can spike.

anima_cost.description: Anima cost to use this technique.

description.description: Description of what this technique does.

source_cantrip.description: The cantrip template this technique was created from, if any.

reach.description:
Positional reach: which positions this technique can target (SAME=melee, ADJACENT=reach, ANY=ranged).

* `same` - Same position
* `adjacent` - Adjacent position
* `any` - Anywhere in room
* `reach_n` - N-hop reach

reach_hops.description: When reach=REACH_N, the maximum number of passable edges BFS may traverse. Ignored for SAME/ADJACENT/ANY.

allowed_viewers.description: Tenures allowed to view this private gallery

PatchedThreadRequest.description:
Serializer for Thread records (Spec A §4.5).

Read: returns level / developed_points / resonance detail for display.
Write: accepts target_kind + target_id + resonance + character_sheet_id to
weave a new thread; target_id is resolved to the typed FK via ``create``
which delegates to ``weave_thread``. ``character_sheet_id`` must identify
a CharacterSheet on an active roster tenure belonging to the requesting
account (staff may pass any sheet).

Read-only computed fields (SerializerMethodFields):
- path_cap: the path-side cap (compute_path_cap)
- anchor_cap: the anchor-side cap (compute_anchor_cap)
- effective_cap: min(path_cap, anchor_cap)

target_kind.description:
Discriminator selecting which target_* FK is populated.

* `TRAIT` - Trait
* `TECHNIQUE` - Technique
* `FACET` - Facet
* `RELATIONSHIP_TRACK` - Relationship Track
* `RELATIONSHIP_CAPSTONE` - Relationship Capstone
* `COVENANT_ROLE` - Covenant Role
* `MANTLE` - Mantle
* `SANCTUM` - Sanctum
* `GIFT` - Gift
* `ORGANIZATION` - Organization

PatchedTransitionRequest.description:
Full serializer for Transition — guarded episode graph edges.

Read-only breadcrumb fields (source_episode_title, target_episode_title)
provide context for the Wave 9 author editor without requiring extra lookups;
they are served free via TransitionViewSet.queryset.select_related.

target_episode.description: May be null when next episode is unauthored (frontier pause).

mode.description:
AUTO fires when eligibility is satisfied. GM_CHOICE requires a Lead GM to pick from the eligible set.

* `auto` - Auto
* `gm_choice` - GM Choice

connection_type.description:
Narrative flavor: THEREFORE / BUT.

* `therefore` - Therefore
* `but` - But

connection_summary.description: Short narrative description of why this transition fires.

PatchedTransitionRequiredOutcomeRequest.description:
Full serializer for TransitionRequiredOutcome.

Records a beat outcome that must be satisfied for this specific transition
to be eligible when the episode is resolved. Stake-level routing (#1770
PR2): when ``stake`` is set the requirement routes on the stake's
StakeOutcome column (``required_stake_column``) and ``required_outcome``
must be blank — exactly one predicate shape per row; validation mirrors
TransitionRequiredOutcome.clean().

required_outcome.description:
Required beat outcome; blank on stake-level rows.

* `unsatisfied` - Unsatisfied
* `success` - Success
* `failure` - Failure
* `expired` - Expired
* `pending_gm_review` - Pending GM review

stake.description: When set, this requirement routes on the stake's StakeOutcome column instead of the beat's outcome.

required_stake_column.description:
Required StakeOutcome column; only with stake set.

* `win` - Win
* `loss` - Loss
* `withdrawal` - Withdrawal

PatchedTreasuredSubjectRequest.description:
Owner-authored ``TreasuredSubject`` CRUD shape.

``owner`` (a ``RosterTenure``) IS client-writable — a player may have
several tenures (characters) — but ``validate()`` rejects any tenure not
belonging to the requesting player, mirroring
``world.consent.serializers``' ``owner_tenure`` validation pattern.

subject_sheet.description: For NPC_FATE / PERSONAL_JEOPARDY subjects. Nulls if the sheet is deleted.

subject_item.description: For ITEM subjects. Nulls if the item instance is deleted/consumed.

subject_society.description: For FACTION subjects (society-level). Nulls if the society is deleted.

subject_organization.description: For FACTION subjects (organization-level). Nulls if the org is deleted.

subject_label.description: Freeform subject name (CUSTOM / CAMPAIGN_TRACK / LOCATION, or flavor).

visibility_mode.description:
Who can see this content.

* `public` - Public
* `private` - Private
* `characters` - Specific Characters
* `groups` - Consent Groups

visible_to_tenures.description: Tenures who can see this (if mode is 'characters').

visible_to_groups.description: Consent groups who can see this (if mode is 'groups').

excluded_tenures.description: Tenures explicitly excluded even if otherwise visible.

PatchedUpdateBulletinPostInputRequest.description:
Input for PATCH /api/table-bulletin-posts/{id}/.

Only the post author (Lead GM of the table) or staff may edit.
No field is required; all are optional.

PatchedUpdateBulletinReplyInputRequest.description:
Input for PATCH /api/table-bulletin-replies/{id}/.

Only the reply author or staff may edit.

PatchedVisibilitySettingsRequest.description:
The active character's own visibility prefs (#1484). Starts with ``appear_offline``.

Read-and-write of quiet/hidden mode for the requesting player's active character. The
fine-grained advanced controls (``show_online_status`` / ``allow_pages`` / ``allow_tells``)
can join this surface later; the model already carries them.

Path.description: Serializer for Path in CG context.

description.description: Lore and flavor text describing this path

stage.description:
Evolution stage (Prospect, Potential, Puissant, etc.)

* `1` - Prospect
* `2` - Potential
* `3` - Puissant
* `4` - True
* `5` - Grand
* `6` - Transcendent

minimum_level.description: Minimum character level to enter this path (1, 3, 6, 11, 16, 21 typical)

aspects.description:
Get aspect names only (weights are staff-only, not exposed to players).

Uses the model's cached_path_aspects property which is populated by
Prefetch(..., to_attr='cached_path_aspects') in the ViewSet. This
avoids SharedMemoryModel cache pollution and provides a single cache
to invalidate when needed.

PathList.description: Lighter serializer for path lists without nested aspects.

description.description: Lore and flavor text describing this path

stage.description:
Evolution stage (Prospect, Potential, Puissant, etc.)

* `1` - Prospect
* `2` - Potential
* `3` - Puissant
* `4` - True
* `5` - Grand
* `6` - Transcendent

minimum_level.description: Minimum character level to enter this path (1, 3, 6, 11, 16, 21 typical)

is_active.description: Whether this path is available for selection

PathOptions.description: Read serializer for GET /path-options/: current path + selectable children.

PathRequest.description: Serializer for Path in CG context.

description.description: Lore and flavor text describing this path

stage.description:
Evolution stage (Prospect, Potential, Puissant, etc.)

* `1` - Prospect
* `2` - Potential
* `3` - Puissant
* `4` - True
* `5` - Grand
* `6` - Transcendent

minimum_level.description: Minimum character level to enter this path (1, 3, 6, 11, 16, 21 typical)

PendingAlteration.description: Read-only serializer for pending alterations shown on character sheet.

character_name.description: Return the primary persona name for the pending's sheet.

tier.description:
Required tier for resolved alteration. Upgradeable via same-scene escalation only.

* `1` - Cosmetic Touch
* `2` - Marked
* `3` - Touched
* `4` - Marked Profoundly
* `5` - Remade

PendingAudereMajoraOffer.description:
Player-facing view of a pending Audere Majora (Crossing) offer (#543). Read-only.

advisory_text is computed live so the corruption warning is always current.
eligible_paths is computed per-object and memoized to avoid N+1 queries.

character_name.description: IC display name via the primary persona.

fired_intensity.description: Runtime intensity of the cast that opened the gate.

soulfray_stage_order.description: Soulfray stage order at fire time (display snapshot).

target_stage_display.description: Human-readable label for the target PathStage.

advisory_text.description: Live corruption advisory; empty string when no stage-3+ corruption.

intended_path_id.description: Return the PathIntent's intended_path_id if it is among eligible paths, else None.

PendingAudereOffer.description:
Player-facing view of a pending Audere offer (#873). Read-only.

advisory_text is computed live (never stored) so the corruption
"character loss" warning is always current.

character_name.description: IC display name via the primary persona.

fired_intensity.description: Runtime intensity of the cast that opened the gate.

soulfray_stage_order.description: Soulfray stage order at fire time (display snapshot).

intensity_bonus.description: Intensity bonus the offer would grant (from the global threshold config).

anima_pool_bonus.description: Anima pool expansion the offer would grant (from the global threshold config).

advisory_text.description: Live corruption advisory; empty string when no stage-3+ corruption.

PendingCrossingOffer.description: Serializer for a pending crossing offer + nested options.

PendingEntryFlourishOffer.description: Player-facing view of a pending entry-flourish offer (#1140). Read-only.

character_sheet_id.description: The character this sheet belongs to

PendingMissionInvite.description: A pending mission invite addressed to the viewer's persona (#2049).

PendingStageAdvanceOffer.description:
Sineater-facing view of a pending stage-advance bonus offer (Task 1.7).

Read-only. Scoped to the authenticated user's character sheets as Sineater.
The ``expires_at`` field lets the UI show a countdown before the offer lapses.

sinner_persona_name.description: Return the Sinner's IC display name via their primary persona.

sinner_corruption_stage.description: The stage the Sinner is currently at when the prompt fires.

commit_units_max.description: Maximum Hollow units the Sineater may commit.

strain_cost_per_unit.description: Strain severity added to the Sineater per committed unit.

expires_at.description: Prompt expires after this time. Stale rows are deleted on next access.

PendingTreasuredSignoffs.description:
Player-safe wire shape for one world.stories.types.PendingTreasuredSignoffs entry (#1853).

Exposes only the requesting player's own beat_id + treasured_subject_ids —
the view-level query already guarantees no other player's data can appear
here (ADR-0033); this serializer adds no fields beyond that.

PermitOfferDetails.description:
Staff CRUD for permit-kind offer details (#1684, closing #728's residual).

``building_kind`` and ``default_approved_wards`` are PK-related writes; the
FE feeds them from the existing ``/api/buildings/building-kinds/`` and
areas list endpoints.

offer.description: The NPCServiceOffer row this details model decorates.

building_kind.description: Which BuildingKind this offer issues permits for. Required for kind=PERMIT offers in Plan 3 (nullable in the schema so existing rows can migrate; runtime issuance asserts non-null).

default_approved_wards.description: Default set of wards the issued permit is valid in. Snapshotted onto BuildingPermitDetails.approved_wards at issuance time.

default_max_target_size.description: Default cap on ``target_size`` for buildings constructed under permits from this offer.

permit_cost_currency.description: Currency cost of the permit (approval fee — distinct from construction cost). Charged to the PC's account at grant time.

PermitOfferDetailsRequest.description:
Staff CRUD for permit-kind offer details (#1684, closing #728's residual).

``building_kind`` and ``default_approved_wards`` are PK-related writes; the
FE feeds them from the existing ``/api/buildings/building-kinds/`` and
areas list endpoints.

offer.description: The NPCServiceOffer row this details model decorates.

building_kind.description: Which BuildingKind this offer issues permits for. Required for kind=PERMIT offers in Plan 3 (nullable in the schema so existing rows can migrate; runtime issuance asserts non-null).

default_approved_wards.description: Default set of wards the issued permit is valid in. Snapshotted onto BuildingPermitDetails.approved_wards at issuance time.

default_max_target_size.description: Default cap on ``target_size`` for buildings constructed under permits from this offer.

permit_cost_currency.description: Currency cost of the permit (approval fee — distinct from construction cost). Charged to the PC's account at grant time.

character_sheet.description: The character sheet this persona belongs to.

is_fake_name.description: True when this persona obscures the character's identity

persona_type.description:
PRIMARY = real identity, ESTABLISHED = persistent alter ego, TEMPORARY = throwaway disguise, ALTERNATE = an alternate self's persona (shapeshift/possession/past-life)

* `primary` - Primary
* `established` - Established
* `temporary` - Temporary
* `alternate` - Alternate

description.description: Physical description text

allow_social_actions.description:
Whether this persona's character may be targeted by social actions.

Mirrors the challenge consent gate (``_tenure_blocks_actor`` with
``category=None``): blocked only when the active tenure's
``SocialConsentPreference`` has ``allow_social_actions=False``. Lets the
scene UI hide/disable the duel-challenge affordance for opted-out
characters (#1181); the backend still enforces the full gate at dispatch.
Defaults to True when there is no tenure or preference row.

PersonaHeat.description:
One warrant row on the viewer's own crime tab — tiers only, never the raw number.

Alleged deeds render as recorded: a false accusation reads the same as a
true one (falsity is emergent, #1765).

PersonaPosition.description: A scene persona and the Position it currently occupies (or null).

PhaseSpec.description: Read-only serializer for a single PhaseSpec dataclass (boss phase budget).

room.description: The room this place belongs to

viewer_is_present.description: Whether one of the requesting account's personas is present at this place (#2156).

room.description: The room this place belongs to

PlacedFixture.description: A comfort fixture placed in the room (removable from the HUD).

PlayerAction.description:
Read-only serializer for PlayerAction — the homogeneous availability descriptor.

Does NOT use DataclassSerializer because PlayerAction contains Django model
instances (check_type, action_template) that DataclassSerializer cannot render.
Uses explicit field definitions with SerializerMethodField for the model instances.

difficulty.description: Return the difficulty enum value string, or None.

PlayerBoundary.description:
Owner-authored ``PlayerBoundary`` CRUD shape.

``owner`` is never client-writable — the viewset sets it from the
requesting player's own ``PlayerData`` on create, so a player can never
author a boundary for anyone else. Mounted behind an owner-scoped
queryset (see ``PlayerBoundaryViewSet.get_queryset``), so this serializer
is only ever handed rows the requester already owns; there is no
non-owner read path through this class.

theme.description: Required for HARD_LINE (the machine-matched category); optional for ADVISORY.

detail.description: Free-text nuance. For HARD_LINE this is staff/audit-only and never surfaced.

visibility_mode.description:
Who can see this content.

* `public` - Public
* `private` - Private
* `characters` - Specific Characters
* `groups` - Consent Groups

visible_to_tenures.description: Tenures who can see this (if mode is 'characters').

visible_to_groups.description: Consent groups who can see this (if mode is 'groups').

excluded_tenures.description: Tenures explicitly excluded even if otherwise visible.

PlayerBoundaryRequest.description:
Owner-authored ``PlayerBoundary`` CRUD shape.

``owner`` is never client-writable — the viewset sets it from the
requesting player's own ``PlayerData`` on create, so a player can never
author a boundary for anyone else. Mounted behind an owner-scoped
queryset (see ``PlayerBoundaryViewSet.get_queryset``), so this serializer
is only ever handed rows the requester already owns; there is no
non-owner read path through this class.

theme.description: Required for HARD_LINE (the machine-matched category); optional for ADVISORY.

detail.description: Free-text nuance. For HARD_LINE this is staff/audit-only and never surfaced.

excluded_tenures.description: Tenures explicitly excluded even if otherwise visible.

PlayerFeedbackCreate.description:
Write serializer - player creates feedback.

Frontend supplies ``reporter_persona``; the serializer validates
that the requesting account currently plays that persona.
``location`` is auto-populated server-side and is not accepted as
input.

reporter_persona.description: The persona the submitter was wearing when they submitted.

description.description: Freeform feedback text from the player.

PlayerFeedbackCreateRequest.description:
Write serializer - player creates feedback.

Frontend supplies ``reporter_persona``; the serializer validates
that the requesting account currently plays that persona.
``location`` is auto-populated server-side and is not accepted as
input.

reporter_persona.description: The persona the submitter was wearing when they submitted.

location.description: Room the submitter was in at the time, if any.

PlayerMedia.description: Serialize media uploaded by a player.

cloudinary_public_id.description: Cloudinary public ID for this media

PlayerReportCreate.description:
Frontend supplies reporter persona + reported persona name (#1279).

The reporter_persona is a PK (the player's own face). The reported persona is
resolved by name — players don't know persona PKs. The reported_account is
derived from the reported persona's current active tenure. If the reported
persona has no current player, the submission is rejected.

reporter_persona.description: The persona the reporter was wearing when submitting.

category.description:
Report category (#1279) — placeholder set, TBD.

* `harassment` - Harassment
* `ooc_abuse` - OOC Abuse
* `red_flag` - FYI / Red Flag
* `other` - Other

behavior_description.description: What the reported persona did. Stub — wording needs design pass.

asked_to_stop.description: Whether the reporter asked the other player to stop the behavior.

blocked_or_muted.description: Whether the reporter blocked or muted as a result.

scene.description: The scene where the behavior occurred, if applicable.

interaction.description: A specific flagged interaction, if applicable.

resolved by name — players don't know persona PKs. The reported_account is

reporter_persona.description: The persona the reporter was wearing when submitting.

interaction.description: A specific flagged interaction, if applicable.

reporter_persona.description: The persona the reporter was wearing when submitting.

reported_account.description: The account behind the reported persona at the time of the incident. Stored directly so staff can query by account without walking the persona chain.

reported_persona.description: The persona of the reported player at the time of the incident.

total_negative_feedback.description: Aggregate negative feedback count from all trust levels

PolishIncrement.description: Per-category polish a decoration template grants on completion.

PortalDestination.description:
One anchor a character could portal-travel to right now (mirrors ``PortalDestination``).

Explicit fields only — never a raw model/dataclass dump, since a locked anchor's
visibility is already gated upstream by
``world.magic.services.portal_travel.portal_destinations`` (#2222 leak table).

PoseEndorsement.description:
Serializer for PoseEndorsement create + read (Spec C Task 23).

Write: accepts ``interaction`` + ``resonance`` PKs from the request body.
The ``endorser_sheet`` is resolved from the requesting account in the
view (``PoseEndorsementViewSet.perform_create``) and injected via
``serializer.save(endorser_sheet=sheet)``.

Read: all fields are present; read-only fields cannot be supplied by the
client.

endorser_sheet.description: The character this sheet belongs to

endorsee_sheet.description: The character this sheet belongs to

persona_snapshot.description: Endorsee's persona at endorsement time — masquerade audit.

PoseEndorsementRequest.description:
Serializer for PoseEndorsement create + read (Spec C Task 23).

Write: accepts ``interaction`` + ``resonance`` PKs from the request body.
The ``endorser_sheet`` is resolved from the requesting account in the
view (``PoseEndorsementViewSet.perform_create``) and injected via
``serializer.save(endorser_sheet=sheet)``.

Read: all fields are present; read-only fields cannot be supplied by the
client.

PositionAdjacencyItem.description:
Read-only serializer for a single PositionAdjacency entry.

Exposes the ADJACENT-reach neighbor graph for one position so the
frontend can pre-filter selectable targets by position before declaring.

PositionEdge.description: Read-only serializer for one PositionEdgeInfo — obstacle/gate visibility (#2006).

PositionNode.description: Read-only serializer for one PositionNode — tactical-map node shape (#2006).

PositionSummary.description: Compact public representation of a Position (id + name).

PowerLedger.description: Serializes a PowerLedger (entries + total) for the cast result payload.

PowerLedgerEntry.description: Serializes a single PowerLedgerEntry for the API payload.

PredicateTypeEnum.description:
* `gm_marked` - GM-marked
* `character_level_at_least` - Character level at least
* `achievement_held` - Achievement held
* `condition_held` - Condition held
* `codex_entry_unlocked` - Codex entry unlocked
* `story_at_milestone` - Referenced story at milestone
* `aggregate_threshold` - Aggregate threshold reached
* `outcome_tier` - Outcome tier (machine-graded)
* `faction_standing_at_least` - Faction standing at least

ProgressionMilestone.description: One milestone entry in a progression stage.

ProgressionStage.description: One stage row in the magic progression dashboard response.

ProgressionUnlockItem.description:
Discriminated list item for purchasable progression unlocks.

Three ``unlock_type`` variants are supported:

- ``class_level`` — purchase a class/level unlock with XP.
- ``thread_xp_lock`` — purchase the next XP-locked boundary on a thread.
- ``skill_breakthrough`` — purchase a skill's XP-boundary breakthrough (#2115).

PromoteGMInputRequest.description:
Validate a staff-driven promotion/demotion before it reaches ``promote_gm`` (#2000).

``context["profile"]`` is the ``GMProfile`` being changed — the view passes
``self.get_object()``. Rejecting the same-level case here means
``promote_gm``'s ``ValueError`` guard is a programmer-error backstop only and
should never fire from user input.

ProposalKindEnum.description:
* `new_situation` - New Situation
* `check_fit` - Check Fit
* `difficulty_guide` - Difficulty Guide
* `pool_guide` - Pool Guide
* `other` - Other

PublicFeedItem.description: One feed row — a deed or a scandal. Read-only; serializes a ``PublicFeedItem`` dataclass.

PullActionContextRequest.description:
Wire shape for the optional ``action_context`` block in a pull preview.

``combat_encounter_id`` and ``target_persona_id`` are consumed by the
preview path — the rest of the fields are accepted for forward-compatibility
with the eventual authoring UI (the pre-commit preview doesn't care about
action_kind or anchors_in_play; the full commit path validates those).

``target_persona_id`` (#2035) names the Persona the pull's action would be
directed at — the same identifier shape as ``ApplicablePullsRequestSerializer``.
The view resolves it to the live ``ObjectDB`` target and feeds it to
``preview_resonance_pull`` so a relationship-bond/Court-regard modulated
amount is visible in the preview, matching what the commit path
(``spend_resonance_for_pull``) would grant. Resolution is gated by
``can_perceive`` (mirrors ``_relationship_pull_would_have_effect`` /
``_court_pull_would_have_effect`` in ``pull_applicability.py``) — this is a
free, repeatable, uncommitted read, so it must not become an oracle for
probing private regard/relationship facts about personas the requester
cannot perceive (see ADR-0086).

SocialConsentCategory.description:
Read-only serializer for social consent categories.

Exposes ``parent`` and ``default_mode`` (#2170) so the frontend can render the category
tree (nested by ``parent``) and show the root's inherited default; ``parent=None`` marks
a root group like "All Antagonism".

description.description: What this category covers.

display_order.description: Sort order in the consent UI.

parent.description: Parent category in the consent tree (#2170); None = a root group. A category with no player rule of its own inherits its parent's effective mode, walking up to the root's default_mode. Lets a player set one node (e.g. 'All Antagonism') and have every category beneath it follow, overriding only where they want an exception.

default_mode.description:
Targeting mode when NOTHING is set anywhere up this category's parent chain. Only consulted on the ROOT of a tree (a category with no parent) — a non-root category inherits its parent instead of consulting its own default_mode. EVERYONE is default-allow; FRIENDS_WHITELIST/RIVALS/ALLOWLIST make it opt-in.

* `everyone` - Everyone
* `all_but_blacklist` - Everyone except my blacklist
* `friends_whitelist` - Friends and my whitelist
* `rivals` - My declared rivals (and whitelist)
* `allowlist` - Allowlist only

action_templates.description: Return the names of action templates tagged with this category.

mode.description:
EVERYONE (anyone), ALL_BUT_BLACKLIST (anyone but this category's blacklist), FRIENDS_WHITELIST (OOC friends + whitelist), or ALLOWLIST (only whitelisted actors).

* `everyone` - Everyone
* `all_but_blacklist` - Everyone except my blacklist

Stake.description:
Full serializer for Stake (#1770 pillar 1).

Template-set path denormalizes subject_kind/severity from the template
(so a later template retune never rewrites live contracts) and validates
the beat's declared risk falls within the template's [min_risk, max_risk]
band (by risk_index). The template-null (CUSTOM) path is gated to staff or
a non-staff GM whose GMLevelCap.allow_custom_stakes is set (see
`_gm_allows_custom_stakes`), mirroring BeatSerializer.validate's risk gate
in style. Any write (create or update) is rejected while the beat carries
an open StakeContractActivation — the lock (#1770 pillar 8).
``outcomes`` (PR2) exposes the read-only resolution audit rows.

template.description: Null only for trust-gated custom stakes.

subject_sheet.description: For NPC_FATE / PERSONAL_JEOPARDY subjects. Nulls if the sheet is deleted.

subject_item.description: For ITEM subjects. Nulls if the item instance is deleted/consumed.

subject_society.description: For FACTION subjects (society-level). Nulls if the society is deleted.

subject_organization.description: For FACTION subjects (organization-level). Nulls if the org is deleted.

subject_label.description: Freeform subject name (CUSTOM / CAMPAIGN_TRACK, or flavor).

player_summary.description: Player-facing line shown at opt-in: what is wagered, how badly.

effective_risk.description:
What Legend actually pays on — see compute_effective_risk.

* `none` - None
* `low` - Low
* `moderate` - Moderate
* `high` - High
* `extreme` - Extreme

is_ready.description: Readiness verdict at activation; False forced effective NONE.

StakeResolution.description:
Full serializer for StakeResolution (#1770 pillar 1).

PR2 adds the writer-payload fields and the pillar-12 no-fiat guard:
sets_subject_lifecycle is only legal for NPC_FATE stakes whose subject
sheet is not player-held; item forfeiture and affection deltas must match
the stake's subject kind. No column-ordering/escalation validation (the
fuse walk measures reachability, not monotonicity).
``reward_lines`` (PR3) exposes the branch's authored win-reward payouts
read-only; they are written via the stake-reward-lines endpoint.

outcome_key.description: Short designer-authored slug naming this branch within its column's polarity (#1760) — e.g. 'destroyed', 'captured', 'given_to_allies'. Blank = the column's single default branch (backward compatible with pre-#1760 content). column stays the coarse WIN/LOSS/WITHDRAWAL polarity every severity/reward/machine-grading rule keys off; outcome_key is a finer dimension within it, not a replacement axis.

consequence_pool.description: Pool to fire when this column resolves (tier-aware).

escalates_to_risk.description:
The risk tier the situation spawned by this branch carries — the fuse mechanic. Blank = no escalation declared.

* `none` - None
* `low` - Low
* `moderate` - Moderate
* `high` - High
* `extreme` - Extreme

narrative_summary.description: What happens in the story when this branch fires (GM-authored).

forfeits_subject_item.description: On fire, soft-forfeit the stake's subject_item (ITEM stakes only).

subject_standing_delta.description: On fire, adjust standing between the stake's subject and each participant persona (#1760). NPC_FATE: adjusts NPCStanding via subject_sheet's primary persona (unchanged pre-#1760 behavior). FACTION: adjusts SocietyReputation or OrganizationReputation (whichever of subject_society/subject_organization is set) — previously a dead FK (subject_society/subject_organization were never read); this is the fix.

sets_subject_lifecycle.description:
On fire, set_lifecycle_state(subject_sheet, value). NPC_FATE only, and only when the subject sheet is not player-held (pillar 12).

* `ALIVE` - Alive
* `CAPTURED` - Captured / Unknown
* `COMA` - Coma
* `RETIRED` - Retired
* `DEAD` - Dead

machine_match_lifecycle_state.description:
On automatic (machine) grading, if the stake's subject_sheet's actual lifecycle_state equals this value, THIS branch is selected over the column's plain default (#1760 — generalizes the old is-dead-only override to the full LifecycleState ladder: ALIVE/CAPTURED/COMA/RETIRED/DEAD). NPC_FATE stakes only — blank means no machine-match, resolve via the plain column default or a GM's Constrained Pick.

* `ALIVE` - Alive
* `CAPTURED` - Captured / Unknown
* `COMA` - Coma
* `RETIRED` - Retired
* `DEAD` - Dead

StakeRewardLine.description:
Full serializer for StakeRewardLine (#1770 PR3 — the contract's win side).

Mirrors StakeResolutionSerializer's gates one hop deeper: the ownership
walk via resolution.stake.beat (create-path enforcement), the two-sided
open-activation lock, the completed-beat refusal, the WIN-column-only
rule, and the sink/resonance shape rule (resonance required iff
sink=RESONANCE; amount >= 1 rides the model validator).
Banding against the tier's reward floor/ceiling is deliberately NOT
rejected here — out-of-band rewards make the contract UNREADY instead
(pillar 7 auto-downgrade); the payout re-checks the band at pay time.

amount.description: Money-equivalent scalar paid to EACH participant (banded by calibration).

resonance.description: Required when sink=RESONANCE; must be null otherwise.

StakeRewardLineRequest.description:
Full serializer for StakeRewardLine (#1770 PR3 — the contract's win side).

Mirrors StakeResolutionSerializer's gates one hop deeper: the ownership

open-activation lock, the completed-beat refusal, the WIN-column-only
rule, and the sink/resonance shape rule (resonance required iff
sink=RESONANCE; amount >= 1 rides the model validator).
Banding against the tier's reward floor/ceiling is deliberately NOT
rejected here — out-of-band rewards make the contract UNREADY instead
(pillar 7 auto-downgrade); the payout re-checks the band at pay time.

amount.description: Money-equivalent scalar paid to EACH participant (banded by calibration).

resonance.description: Required when sink=RESONANCE; must be null otherwise.

StoryProtectedSubject.description:
Full serializer for StoryProtectedSubject (#2001 Task 6).

Enforces the model's exactly-one-subject invariant here (DRF serializers
never call ``Model.clean()``) and the story-ownership gate on create/update
— ``IsProtectedSubjectStoryOwnerOrStaff.has_object_permission`` alone
cannot cover create, since DRF never calls object-level permissions for a
row that does not exist yet.

beat.description: For beat-level refinement: protection applies only while this beat is unsatisfied. Null = story-level (whole arc). SET_NULL (not CASCADE) — a deleted beat must not silently erase the protection; it degrades to story-level instead.

notes.description: GM notes on why this subject is critical. GM-only; never serialized to outsiders.

SystemErrorReportDetail.description:
Staff read + status-update view of an auto-captured error (#1164).

Everything is read-only except ``status`` — the report is system-authored, so staff
only triage it (OPEN -> REVIEWED / DISMISSED), never edit its captured contents. The
same serializer drives retrieve and update, matching the BugReport pattern.

signature.description: Dedup hash (exception type + originating in-app frame).

label.description: Where it happened (the hook / context label).

traceback.description: Full formatted traceback of the first occurrence.

actor_persona.description: The persona acting when first captured, if any.

actor_persona_name.description: The acting persona's name, or ``None`` — actor_persona is nullable.

github_issue_number.description: GitHub issue number, once staff have filed one from this report (#1164).

github_issue_url.description: Link to the filed GitHub issue (empty until filed).

issue_draft.description: The redacted, staff-editable draft for the File GitHub issue dialog.

Everything is read-only except ``status`` — the report is system-authored, so staff
only triage it (OPEN -> REVIEWED / DISMISSED), never edit its captured contents. The
same serializer drives retrieve and update, matching the BugReport pattern.

replies.description: Return cached replies (from Prefetch to_attr) or query the DB.

name.description: Display name of the card, e.g. 'The Fool' or 'Three of Swords'.

arcana_type.description:
Whether this card is Major or Minor Arcana.

* `major` - Major Arcana
* `minor` - Minor Arcana

suit.description:
Suit for Minor Arcana cards. Null for Major Arcana.

* `swords` - Swords
* `cups` - Cups
* `wands` - Wands
* `coins` - Coins

rank.description: Card rank: 0-21 for Major Arcana, 1-14 for Minor Arcana.

latin_name.description: Latin name for Major Arcana cards, used as upright surname.

description.description: Flavor text or thematic description of the card.

description_reversed.description: Description when the card is drawn reversed.

Technique.description: Serializer for Technique records with intensity and control stats.

name.description: Name of the technique (not unique - different characters can have same name).

gift.description: The gift this technique belongs to.

style.description: The style of this technique (restricted by Path).

effect_type.description: The type of effect this technique produces.

level.description: The level of this technique (determines tier).

intensity.description: Base power of the technique. Determines damage and effect strength.

control.description: Base safety/precision. When intensity exceeds control at runtime, effects become unpredictable and anima cost can spike.

anima_cost.description: Anima cost to use this technique.

description.description: Description of what this technique does.

source_cantrip.description: The cantrip template this technique was created from, if any.

target_type.description:
Per-technique target cardinality (how many / how selected). Relationship (self/ally/enemy) is derived from condition target_kinds + hostility, not stored here.

* `self` - Self
* `single` - Single Target
* `area` - Area
* `filtered_group` - Filtered Group

reach.description:
Positional reach: which positions this technique can target (SAME=melee, ADJACENT=reach, ANY=ranged).

* `same` - Same position
* `adjacent` - Adjacent position
* `any` - Anywhere in room
* `reach_n` - N-hop reach

reach_hops.description: When reach=REACH_N, the maximum number of passable edges BFS may traverse. Ignored for SAME/ADJACENT/ANY.

TechniqueCastCreateRequest.description: Validate input for the standalone technique cast endpoint.

TechniqueReachEnum.description:

name.description: Name of the technique (not unique - different characters can have same name).

gift.description: The gift this technique belongs to.

style.description: The style of this technique (restricted by Path).

effect_type.description: The type of effect this technique produces.

level.description: The level of this technique (determines tier).

intensity.description: Base power of the technique. Determines damage and effect strength.

control.description: Base safety/precision. When intensity exceeds control at runtime, effects become unpredictable and anima cost can spike.

anima_cost.description: Anima cost to use this technique.

description.description: Description of what this technique does.

source_cantrip.description: The cantrip template this technique was created from, if any.

reach.description:
Positional reach: which positions this technique can target (SAME=melee, ADJACENT=reach, ANY=ranged).

* `same` - Same position
* `adjacent` - Adjacent position
* `any` - Anywhere in room
* `reach_n` - N-hop reach

reach_hops.description: When reach=REACH_N, the maximum number of passable edges BFS may traverse. Ignored for SAME/ADJACENT/ANY.

description.description: Description of this technique style.

Thread.description:
Serializer for Thread records (Spec A §4.5).

Read: returns level / developed_points / resonance detail for display.
Write: accepts target_kind + target_id + resonance + character_sheet_id to
weave a new thread; target_id is resolved to the typed FK via ``create``
which delegates to ``weave_thread``. ``character_sheet_id`` must identify
a CharacterSheet on an active roster tenure belonging to the requesting
account (staff may pass any sheet).

Read-only computed fields (SerializerMethodFields):
- path_cap: the path-side cap (compute_path_cap)
- anchor_cap: the anchor-side cap (compute_anchor_cap)
- effective_cap: min(path_cap, anchor_cap)

target_kind.description:
Discriminator selecting which target_* FK is populated.

* `TRAIT` - Trait
* `TECHNIQUE` - Technique
* `FACET` - Facet
* `RELATIONSHIP_TRACK` - Relationship Track
* `RELATIONSHIP_CAPSTONE` - Relationship Capstone
* `COVENANT_ROLE` - Covenant Role
* `MANTLE` - Mantle
* `SANCTUM` - Sanctum
* `GIFT` - Gift
* `ORGANIZATION` - Organization

level.description: Current level on the internal scale (multiples of 10).

developed_points.description: Permanent points; advances level via ThreadLevelUnlock entries.

path_cap.description: Return the path-side cap for this thread's owner.

anchor_cap.description: Return the anchor-side cap for this thread.

retired_at.description: Set when owner soft-retires this thread; retired threads are excluded from list/detail views and from all pull / passive paths.

ThreadApplicability.description:
One row in the applicable-pulls response.

Response shape: ``{thread_id, applicable, inapplicable_reason}``.
``inapplicable_reason`` is null when ``applicable`` is true.

requesting account owns (staff may pass any sheet).

effective_xp_cost_for_viewer.description:
Compute the Path-multiplied XP cost for the requesting learner.

Returns the integer cost when the viewer has exactly one active tenure
OR provides a ``learner_sheet_id`` query param to disambiguate.
Returns ``None`` for ambiguous (multi-tenure, no key) or no-tenure cases.

Uses ``_get_viewer_sheets`` so the tenant-resolution query fires only once
per list response, not once per row.

pitch.description: Teacher's narrative pitch for this offer.

gold_cost.description: Gold price the teacher charges (XP cost stays on the unlock).

description.description: Player-facing description of this tradition's philosophy and practices.

is_active.description: Whether this tradition is currently available for selection.

sort_order.description: Display ordering within lists (lower numbers appear first).

codex_entry_ids.description:
Get codex entry IDs granted by this tradition.

Read from the ``cached_codex_grants`` attr populated by the
``Beginnings.cached_beginning_traditions`` prefetch. Same data for
every caller (no per-request filter), so attaching to the shared
Tradition instance is safe.

required_distinction_id.description:
Get the required distinction ID from the BeginningTradition context.

The view computes a ``{tradition_id: BeginningTradition}`` dict per
request and passes it via context. We do NOT attach the BT row to
``obj`` (a SharedMemoryModel ``Tradition``) via ``Prefetch(to_attr=)``
because that attribute would persist across requests with different
``beginning_id`` values and leak filtered data between users.

provide context for the Wave 9 author editor without requiring extra lookups;
they are served free via TransitionViewSet.queryset.select_related.

target_episode.description: May be null when next episode is unauthored (frontier pause).

target_episode_title.description: Return target episode title, or None when target is null (frontier).

mode.description:
AUTO fires when eligibility is satisfied. GM_CHOICE requires a Lead GM to pick from the eligible set.

* `auto` - Auto
* `gm_choice` - GM Choice

connection_type.description:

* `therefore` - Therefore
* `but` - But

connection_summary.description: Short narrative description of why this transition fires.

TransitionRequiredOutcome.description:
Full serializer for TransitionRequiredOutcome.

Records a beat outcome that must be satisfied for this specific transition
to be eligible when the episode is resolved. Stake-level routing (#1770
PR2): when ``stake`` is set the requirement routes on the stake's
StakeOutcome column (``required_stake_column``) and ``required_outcome``
must be blank — exactly one predicate shape per row; validation mirrors
TransitionRequiredOutcome.clean().

required_outcome.description:
Required beat outcome; blank on stake-level rows.

* `unsatisfied` - Unsatisfied
* `success` - Success
* `failure` - Failure
* `expired` - Expired
* `pending_gm_review` - Pending GM review

stake.description: When set, this requirement routes on the stake's StakeOutcome column instead of the beat's outcome.

required_stake_column.description:
Required StakeOutcome column; only with stake set.

* `win` - Win
* `loss` - Loss
* `withdrawal` - Withdrawal

travel_modes.description: List of TravelMode values that can use this hub (e.g. ["LAND", "SEA"]). A pier serves SEA; a city gate serves LAND.

is_transit_stop.description: If True, this hub appears as a waypoint in route BFS. If False, it's an embarkation/disembarkation point only.

ship_type_id.description: If set, this method is for a specific ship type. Speed overridden by effective_handling().

is_default.description: The method every character has by default (On Foot).

TreasuredSignoff.description:
A player's pre-scene sign-off to stake one of their treasured subjects.

``player_data`` is never client-writable — the viewset sets it from the
requesting player, so a player can only sign off as themselves.
``treasured_subject`` must belong to one of the requesting player's own
tenures (validated below) — a player cannot sign off on someone else's
treasured subject.

``treasured_subject`` must belong to one of the requesting player's own
tenures (validated below) — a player cannot sign off on someone else's
treasured subject.

TreasuredSubject.description:
Owner-authored ``TreasuredSubject`` CRUD shape.

``owner`` (a ``RosterTenure``) IS client-writable — a player may have
several tenures (characters) — but ``validate()`` rejects any tenure not
belonging to the requesting player, mirroring
``world.consent.serializers``' ``owner_tenure`` validation pattern.

subject_sheet.description: For NPC_FATE / PERSONAL_JEOPARDY subjects. Nulls if the sheet is deleted.

subject_item.description: For ITEM subjects. Nulls if the item instance is deleted/consumed.

subject_society.description: For FACTION subjects (society-level). Nulls if the society is deleted.

subject_organization.description: For FACTION subjects (organization-level). Nulls if the org is deleted.

subject_label.description: Freeform subject name (CUSTOM / CAMPAIGN_TRACK / LOCATION, or flavor).

visibility_mode.description:

* `characters` - Specific Characters
* `groups` - Consent Groups

visible_to_tenures.description: Tenures who can see this (if mode is 'characters').

visible_to_groups.description: Consent groups who can see this (if mode is 'groups').

excluded_tenures.description: Tenures explicitly excluded even if otherwise visible.

TreatmentCandidate.description:
One candidate treatment a helper may offer a target persona (#1486).

Describes the shape returned by ``TreatmentCandidateViewSet.list`` so
drf-spectacular emits a real response body instead of ``content?: never``.
The view still returns the same hand-built dict; this serializer is
schema metadata only.

``target_effect`` is a discriminated union: a ``ConditionInstance``
serialization when ``target_effect_type == 'condition'``, or a
``PendingAlteration`` serialization when ``target_effect_type ==
'alteration'``. Modeled as a ``DictField`` because the two target
serializers live in different apps; ``target_effect_type`` is the
discriminator the client switches on.

TreatmentCandidateResponse.description:
Envelope returned by the treatments discovery endpoint (#1486).

``many=False`` overrides drf-spectacular's list-view heuristic: the
endpoint lives on a ``list`` action, so the generator would otherwise
wrap this single envelope object in an array. The runtime returns one
``{"candidates": [...], "scene_id": int}`` object, not an array of them.

TreatmentTemplate.description:
Read-only serializer for treatment template definitions.

Surfaces the authored recipe for attempting to treat a condition or
pending alteration (costs, bond requirement, scene requirement) so the
web Treat panel can render the candidate list returned by
``get_treatment_candidates``.

TreatmentTemplateTargetKindEnum.description:
* `primary` - Primary condition severity
* `aftermath` - Aftermath child condition severity
* `pending_alteration` - Pending alteration tier

_CategoryPolish.description: One polish category's value + derived tier label for a building.

_Deed.description: LegendEntry summary for the recent-deeds list.

_DuelParticipantIdentity.description:
Compact identity for one side of a DuelChallenge (CharacterSheet id + name).

Note: CharacterSheet's primary key is the ``character`` OneToOneField, so
there is no ``.id`` attribute — ``source="pk"`` reads ``.pk`` explicitly.

name.description: Return the character's display name from CharacterSheet.character.db_key.

_Fame.description: Persona's fame state for the renown tab header.

_NearXPLockProspect.description: One entry in the near-xp-lock list returned by ThreadHubSummaryView.

_OwnedDwelling.description:
One building the persona owns, with polish breakdown + condition label.

The renown payload is viewable by any authenticated player, so this
carries only the public fiction: the qualitative condition-tier label
(#1930, ADR-0031). Arrears / miss counters / mothball state are
owner-only and surface through the owner action family instead.

_PrestigeBreakdown.description: Five-axis breakdown of the persona's total_prestige.

_RenownCardFame.description:
Fame block on the card — tier label only.

The full Renown tab exposes numeric fame_points + multiplier for
the player's own personas; foreign personas show just the tier
label (the spec is explicit about no numeric reveal).

_ResonanceBalance.description: One resonance balance entry returned by ThreadHubSummaryView.

_SocietyReputation.description: One reputation entry: society + tier label (no raw number).

_TenantedRoom.description:
One room the persona tenants — polish breakdown only, no upkeep/dormancy.

Upkeep + dormancy are building-level concepts; rooms don't carry
them directly. The room's containing building's upkeep state shows
up in ``owned_dwellings`` separately (when the persona owns it).

_WeavableTechnique.description: One entry in the weavable_techniques list.

_WeavableTrait.description: One entry in the weavable_traits list.
